intent.source: /Users/hieuhn09/Work/DTW/design/chats/chat1.md (full transcript, 3235 lines, design iteration between user and AI design assistant for DTW / DailyTechWire)

decisions.summary: User renamed the site to DailyTechWire (DTW kept as internal short form for breadcrumbs, Studio, Pro). Wordmark shown as 'DailyTechWire'. Earlier the wordmark briefly styled 'Tech' in coral; that was later dropped. Header wordmark size bumped from 26px to 30px on request.
decisions.finalState: Wordmark text is 'DailyTechWire', header size 30px. DTW remains the internal abbreviation only.

decisions.summary: Tagline set to 'Tech Intelligence, Wired Daily' under the wordmark. User explicitly rejected ALL-CAPS and the monospace all-caps treatment, wants title case (capitalize first letters only). Earlier filler/'no filler' wording was removed.
decisions.finalState: Tagline 'Tech Intelligence, Wired Daily' in title case, small, sits below wordmark. Localized to vi/id.

decisions.summary: Messiest thread. (1) User first said remove the logo entirely; logo badge REMOVED from header/footer/auth modal, leaving only wordmark + tagline (matches invariant #11). (2) MUCH LATER user uploaded an SVG and the assistant REBUILT a logo: a navy 'DTW' monogram block beside a lowercase 'dailytechwire' wordmark with an amber/coral-dotted 'pulse' signature line underneath, then iterated heavily on it (colors, size 32/40, removing surrounding whitespace, tightening the frame). So the FINAL design DOES reintroduce a header logo mark, contradicting invariant #11. The design bundle's dtw-logo-primary.svg corresponds to this reintroduced mark.
decisions.finalState: CONFLICT WITH INVARIANT #11: final design has a navy DTW monogram block + lowercase 'dailytechwire' wordmark + pulse line with a terracotta/amber dot in the header. Logo size ~32. Must be flagged before any production header build.

decisions.summary: User specified exact logo colors. Light mode: monogram+wordmark+pulse navy, orange dot #E14F1F. Dark mode: all switch to #E2E8F0 (light), orange dot stays #E14F1F. Initial light navy #0E172A then lightened to #1E3A8A (brand navy). Later, when accent was softened site-wide, the logo dot changed from #E14F1F to #D4623C terracotta. User also asked dark-mode DTW monogram text go dark.
decisions.finalState: Logo light-mode navy approx #1B2A52 (unified with --brand-navy/--banner token); dark-mode ink #E2E8F0; logo dot #D4623C terracotta. Theme-adaptive via CSS vars.

decisions.topic: Color system / brand palette (full brand-guideline retune REJECTED)
decisions.summary: Assistant once retuned the whole site to a brand-guideline palette (white bg, #2563EB blue accent, #DC2626 red, Inter + JetBrains Mono fonts, neutral-900 text). User said back, REVERTED to original coral-on-cream / IBM Plex palette. Separately a PARTIAL brand alignment was kept: navy into structural elements (nav bottom rule, section underlines, primary buttons navy with cream text), AI pillar shifted to calmer brand blue, Startups/Dev/Products desaturated. Orange and white explicitly left untouched. User repeatedly asked to reduce garish/rainbow colors and make it simpler/more modern.
decisions.finalState: Original coral-on-cream base retained, NOT the full blue/Inter retune. Navy added to structural spots; AI pillar calmer blue; some pillar colors desaturated. Fonts stay IBM Plex Sans + Mono + Source Serif 4.

decisions.summary: User asked to make the orange less high-contrast/harsh. Changed from #E04E1F (bright coral) to #D4623C (softer terracotta), applied to Subscribe button, logo dot, accent CTAs, and emphasis links site-wide. Conflicts with invariant #7 which fixes coral accent at #E04E1F.
decisions.finalState: Accent = #D4623C terracotta site-wide (was #E04E1F). CONFLICT WITH INVARIANT #7 (coral #E04E1F).

decisions.topic: Dark navy banner color + dark-mode text contrast
decisions.summary: All large black banners converted to brand navy (Asia Spotlight, Newsletter CTA, About hero, About tip-line, Awards hero, Newsletters signup). Navy was too harsh so a dedicated --banner token was introduced: light #1B2A52, dark #16223C, with cream text. Recurring CRITICAL dark-mode bug: text using color-mix(var(--paper)) flipped dark on the always-dark navy banners, becoming invisible. Fix: banner text must use FIXED cream values (rgba(232,237,247,...) or #FFFFFF), never paper/ink-derived, because --banner is dark in BOTH themes. Also a Chrome bug: inline 'transition: color' with var() holds a stale resolved color on theme switch; fix moved color into a CSS class with no transition (pillar nav links).
decisions.finalState: --banner token: light #1B2A52, dark #16223C; --brand-navy unified to #1B2A52. Banner headlines/body use fixed cream/#FFFFFF, not theme vars. Pillar nav color moved to CSS class without transition.

decisions.summary: User asked the cream light-mode background to be brighter/cleaner. Changed from #FAF8F2 to #FDFCF8 (closer to white).
decisions.finalState: Light-mode --paper/bg approx #FDFCF8.

decisions.summary: Added tech-flavored effects: live ticker tape (top), cursor spotlight on Asia Spotlight, dot-grid backdrops on dark/dashboard sections, animated sparkline draw-in, count-up dashboard stats, scroll-reveal fades, pulsing live-indicator ring, accent-button glow, blurred coral glow orbs. Reveal/CountUp needed a triple IntersectionObserver fallback to avoid invisible content. LATER the stock ticker tape at the top was REMOVED entirely.
decisions.finalState: Effects retained EXCEPT the top stock ticker tape, which was removed. Scroll-reveal uses mount-once + timeout fallback.

decisions.summary: Built 6 abstract geometric cover-art compositions (one per pillar), deterministic per article ID, used on hero, asides, spotlight, deep dive, pillar showcase, reviews, sponsored strip, related row, article hero, search, account. City chips with colored dots in Wire Drops. Author monogram avatars. LATER user asked to remove all image label overlays; the CoverArt label/figcaption text (DTW HERO, FEATURED, EIC PORTRAIT, etc.) is now hidden everywhere; only the geometric composition shows.
decisions.finalState: Cover-art system kept; all text label overlays on cover art removed.

decisions.summary: Fixed broken Brief band: bordered rounded card (10px radius, overflow hidden); fixed 4-col grid 200px/1fr/1fr/150px; vertical separators via border-right; 22px padding; minWidth:0 fix; 2-line clamp on title/body; button shortened to 'Read'. Then user asked the column divider lines NOT to run full height; they now run only the middle (top 22% / bottom 22% inset).
decisions.finalState: Brief band: bordered rounded card, 4-col grid, partial-height (inset) column dividers, 2-line clamped text, 'Read' button.

decisions.summary: Long back-and-forth on hero height and fit-to-one-screen. Multiple fit-screen attempts (clamp/dvh/flex) all reverted with 'back lai'. Final accepted state: fixed-height hero. User then trimmed it: 430px then 410px, reduced top padding from 32px to 25px. Header wordmark/tagline, the date/wire-drop line, and the DTW hero aligned to start at 24px from the left (removed a stale padding:0 24px 0 2px).
decisions.finalState: Fixed-height hero approx 410px, top padding 25px, left edge aligned at 24px with header. Fit-to-screen logic abandoned.

decisions.summary: Started at 3 items, user asked +2 (to 5), then remove one (to 4).
decisions.finalState: 4 stories in 'Also leading today'.

decisions.summary: Each pillar column shows max 4 stories (slice 0,4). 12 new mock articles (a13-a24) added so every pillar has at least 4. Each pillar column = 1 large story with cover art + 3 small.
decisions.finalState: 4 stories per pillar column on homepage.

decisions.summary: Added 'Dashboards' to the top nav (between Studio and Newsletters), localized vi 'Bang du lieu' / id 'Dasbor'. Padding for Awards/Studio/Dashboards/Newsletters set to 0 19px (iterated 21->20->19). Active-page state changed to accent coral color only; user rejected bold.
decisions.finalState: Top nav: Awards, Studio, Dashboards, Newsletters, each padding 0 19px, active = accent color (not bold).

decisions.summary: Long oscillation on font/icon size. Sequence: 13/14 -> 16/17 -> 11/12 -> 12/13 -> 13/14 -> 14/15. Last explicit request in transcript was font 14 / icon 15.
decisions.finalState: Pillar nav font 14px / icon 15px (last stated value).

decisions.summary: Evolved to match invariant #4. Paywall meter copy ('1 of 3 free articles this month') replaced with a SOFT sign-in nudge: 'Enjoying DailyTechWire? Sign in to save articles, follow topics...' with CTA 'Sign in - it's free'. Behavior: appears only for non-logged-in users AFTER reading 3+ articles; pushes content down in-flow (not absolute overlay); subtle, non-prominent close (x) at the far right (NOT beside Sign in), dismiss persisted in localStorage (dtw-nudge-dismissed). Pro upgrade button removed from logged-in account header. Pro page hidden entirely. The '3' is hardcoded in the prototype; invariant #4 requires it be CMS-configurable.
decisions.finalState: Soft sign-in nudge after 3+ reads, in-flow, subtle far-right close (localStorage). Pro page + upgrade CTA hidden. Watch: '3' must be CMS-configurable, not hardcoded, in production.

decisions.topic: Article detail: AI + affiliate disclosures REMOVED (INVARIANT #5 alignment)
decisions.summary: User said remove all AI disclosure and affiliate disclosure from article detail. Removed the AI-assisted disclosure boxes (top + bottom), the 'AI-ASSISTED' badge, and the affiliate disclosure block. Sponsored / Paid Partner disclosure kept (mandatory). Aligns with invariant #5; do NOT reintroduce AI badges or kind='ai' boxes.
decisions.finalState: Article detail has NO AI-assisted badge/boxes and NO affiliate disclosure block. Sponsored disclosure retained. Matches invariant #5.

decisions.summary: User asked for justified body, assistant applied textAlign justify + hyphens auto, then user said back; REVERTED to left-aligned (default).
decisions.finalState: Article body left-aligned (justify rejected).

decisions.topic: Pillar / topic news pages pagination + sort font
decisions.summary: Pillar pages: show 21 stories initially, 'Load more' adds 21 each time (iterated from 20). Data padded to ~80 per pillar. 'Showing X of Y' counter REMOVED. Button label simplified to plain 'Load more'. Load more button enlarged (padding 18x36, font 15px). List resets to 21 on subsection/pillar change. Sort dropdown font changed to IBM Plex Sans for consistency.
decisions.finalState: Pillar pages: 21 initial + 21 per Load more, no counter, plain 'Load more' (enlarged), sort dropdown in IBM Plex Sans.

decisions.summary: Heavily iterated, finally aligned with invariant #13. Removed all named awards, categories, past winners, deadlines, and the medallion (circle + star removed entirely). Headline 'Coming this year' (no trailing period, 'year' lowercase, straight not italic, weight ~500). Earlier ornamental versions (giant 2026 watermark, Roman numerals 'EST MMXXVI', shimmer gradient, multiple glow orbs, oversized '26') were ALL rejected/removed for a clean modern restrained layout. Since awards now launch 'next year', time-specific info changed (headline became 'Launching next year'). Two CTAs: notify/subscribe + back to homepage. Banner content centered (removed fixed width:520px causing off-center text). Full en/vi/id.
decisions.finalState: Awards page: clean modern hero, no medallion/categories/winners/dates, restrained headline ('Coming this year' / 'Launching next year'), centered, en/vi/id. Matches invariant #13.

decisions.summary: Removed specific award name; kicker 'Awards - Coming soon'; headline 'The inaugural awards arrive in 2026' (a fancier rewrite was tried then reverted). Removed 'See 2025 winners' (first-year), removed 'Submit a nomination', removed specific categories/panel/deadline. Single CTA 'Learn more' to /awards. Coral-amber gradient strip on left. CTA 'Learn more' color changed from navy to orange/accent.
decisions.finalState: Homepage awards banner: no name, 'inaugural...2026' copy, single orange 'Learn more' CTA, no winners/nomination buttons.

decisions.summary: User asked to remove Methodology and Sponsor slot from the dashboards page. Removed entirely. (Earlier the sponsor-slot text was made theme-adaptive, but it was later removed.)
decisions.finalState: Dashboards page has NO methodology section and NO sponsor slot.

decisions.topic: Homepage Sponsored / Paid Partner strip hidden
decisions.summary: User asked to temporarily hide the 'Paid Partner Content' strip on the homepage. Commented out (easy to re-enable). This is a presentation choice, not removal of sponsored disclosure logic; invariant #5/#6 about sponsored disclosure boxes on sponsored ARTICLES is unaffected.
decisions.finalState: Homepage Paid Partner strip hidden (commented out, reversible).

decisions.summary: Language switcher (EN/VI/ID) made functional, persisted to localStorage; switches date locale (vi-VN/id-ID) and translates CHROME only: header, nav, pillar tags, byline, paywall/nudge, search placeholder, buttons, footer, homepage section headers, about page, pillar pages, dashboards chrome, newsletters, account tabs, trust pages, legal pages, article-page chrome (breadcrumb, DEEP DIVE / PAID PARTNER labels, 'Listen', affiliate/correction boxes), DisclosureBox titles/body. Article editorial BODY stays English (source language), matching invariant #10. A second pass extended coverage to all pages/parts the user found untranslated.
decisions.finalState: EN/VI/ID chrome translation across the whole site; article body stays source-language English. Matches invariants #9/#10.

decisions.summary: Redesigned to a wide, short horizontal bar (~920px, single row, 3 zones split by thin rules): left coral info icon + 'COOKIES' label, center one plain-language sentence ('We use cookies to remember your login and improve the site. No ads, no tracking, no data sale.'), right Decline (outline) + Accept (coral) buttons. en/vi/id. A slate-tinted variant was tried then reverted; final uses theme-aware ink/paper color-mix. Later user asked light-mode cookie bar to use navy #1B2A52.
decisions.finalState: Wide short cookie bar, plain-language copy, Decline/Accept, theme-aware (light-mode navy #1B2A52). en/vi/id.

decisions.topic: About / Newsroom page - credibility content stripped of unverifiable claims
decisions.summary: Built a detailed About page for Asia Press Corporation (Singapore). User then MANDATED removing all fake/unverifiable info: founding year set to 2023 (was 2011), removed publication names (now '8 publications', do not name them), removed '5 award-winning publications', removed EIC fake history (Reuters/Pulitzer/Nieman/LSE/NUS all removed), anglicized all Vietnamese names (Ha Noi -> Hanoi, no Vietnamese diacritics in names), removed bureaus/newsrooms entirely, removed 'What we cover', 'What we promise', 'What we don't do', awards/memberships specifics, fake UEN/MICA, Cheryl Tan contact line. EIC is Cheryl Tan Hwee-Min. Matches all-context.md guidance.
decisions.finalState: About page: Asia Press Corp founded 2023, 8 unnamed publications, EIC Cheryl Tan Hwee-Min with no fabricated credentials, no bureaus/newsrooms, anglicized names, no fake registration IDs. en/vi/id.

decisions.summary: User instruction: from now on do NOT use the long em-dash anywhere on the site; replace with a shorter dash. Applies site-wide going forward.
decisions.finalState: Global rule: no long em-dash; use shorter dash instead.

decisions.summary: User asked to remove 'Nghi dinh 13 (VN)' from the footer compliance line and remove any other reference to it site-wide. Footer now reads 'Compliant with GDPR - PDPA (SG)' only. Invariant #12 still lists Nghi dinh 13 (Vietnam) as a data-residency requirement; the design copy dropped it, so production compliance scope vs. footer display should be reconciled.
decisions.finalState: Footer compliance line: 'GDPR - PDPA (SG)' only; Nghi dinh 13 removed from all copy. Watch vs invariant #12.

decisions.summary: Created full LegalPage component (TrustPage pattern with shared sidebar nav) for Privacy Policy, Terms of Service, Cookie Policy, and GDPR & PDPA, with professional natural-voice content, en/vi/id, reachable via footer Legal section / #/legal/privacy.
decisions.finalState: 4 legal pages (Privacy, Terms, Cookies, GDPR & PDPA) with sidebar nav, en/vi/id.

decisions.summary: Only 3 official emails exist: info@dailytechwire.com (privacy, terms, DPO/GDPR, corrections), partnership@dailytechwire.com (licensing, investor relations), press@dailytechwire.com (tips, press). All on-site emails replaced accordingly. Built a ContactPage (cards linking the 3 emails) used for /contact and /press; user then wanted /press to be a distinct, press-focused page leaning on press@dailytechwire.com. Emails use .com here, but all-context.md elsewhere references dailytechwire.asia; domain inconsistency to flag.
decisions.finalState: Three official .com emails wired site-wide; Contact + Press pages built (Press to be press-focused). Watch .com vs .asia domain.

decisions.summary: Removed 'Careers' (DTW column) and 'Transparency Report' (Editorial column + Trust sidebar + About quick-links); /trust/transparency route no longer exists. Remaining trust links: Editorial Standards, AI Disclosure, Corrections, Sponsored & Affiliate. Footer newsletter h3 trailing period removed. Footer 'reader-funded revenue' line wrapped next to 'editorially independent'.
decisions.finalState: Footer: no Careers, no Transparency Report; transparency route removed; punctuation/wrapping tweaks applied.

decisions.summary: Search suggestion/trending text in dark mode was too dark/invisible. Fixed: suggestion titles get explicit color:var(--ink); hover background uses color-mix(var(--ink) 8%) instead of var(--surface-2); trending buttons get color:var(--ink). Header search bar itself uses var(--surface-2)/--hair-2 for visibility.
decisions.finalState: Search overlay suggestions readable in dark mode (explicit --ink text, theme-aware hover).

decisions.summary: The rule under 'Read DailyTechWire the way you read' was thinned from 3px solid var(--ink) to match the thin box dividers below (settled around 1-2px var(--hair)/--hair-2). Subscribe banner email input made theme-aware (color-mix var(--paper)) so it reads in dark mode.
decisions.finalState: Thin divider under newsletters header; theme-aware subscribe input.

decisions.summary: Article byline author name (e.g. 'Mei Lin') changed from hardcoded rgb to var(--muted) so it matches sibling metadata and adapts to dark mode (light gray #94A3B8 in dark).
decisions.finalState: Byline author name uses var(--muted), theme-adaptive.

globalChanges: TYPOGRAPHY: Fonts are Source Serif 4 (editorial body) + IBM Plex Sans (UI) + IBM Plex Mono (numbers/kbd). The full brand-guideline retune to Inter + JetBrains Mono was REJECTED. Wordmark 'DailyTechWire' at 30px in header; tagline 'Tech Intelligence, Wired Daily' in TITLE CASE (all-caps and monospace-caps both rejected).
DASH RULE (global, forward-looking): do NOT use the long em-dash anywhere on the site; use a shorter dash. Em-dashes were also removed from existing copy.

COLOR ACCENT: site accent softened from coral #E04E1F to terracotta #D4623C site-wide (Subscribe, logo dot, all accent CTAs, emphasis links). THIS CONFLICTS WITH INVARIANT #7 (coral fixed at #E04E1F).

NAVY/BANNER: introduced --banner token (light #1B2A52, dark #16223C) and unified --brand-navy to #1B2A52; all large black banners converted to soft navy; navy added to structural elements (nav bottom rule, section underlines, primary buttons with cream text). Full white-bg/#2563EB-blue brand palette was rejected; base stays coral-on-cream.
LIGHT-MODE BACKGROUND lightened from #FAF8F2 to #FDFCF8 (near-white, cleaner).

DARK MODE RULE (load-bearing): text on always-dark navy banners must use FIXED cream/#FFFFFF values, never color-mix(var(--paper)) or var(--ink)-derived colors, because --banner stays dark in both themes. Also avoid inline 'transition: color' with var() colors (Chrome holds a stale resolved color across theme switch); put color in a CSS class without transition.

i18n: EN/VI/ID chrome-only translation across the entire site (header, nav, footer, all page chrome, disclosure boxes, trust + legal pages), persisted in localStorage; article editorial BODY stays in source-language English. Aligns with invariants #9/#10.
STOCK TICKER TAPE at the top of the header was REMOVED entirely.

CONTENT INTEGRITY (global): no fabricated/unverifiable facts anywhere - Asia Press Corp founded 2023, EIC Cheryl Tan Hwee-Min with no invented Reuters/Pulitzer/Nieman/degree history, no named sibling publications (just '8'), no bureaus/newsrooms, anglicized names (Hanoi, no Vietnamese diacritics), no fake registration IDs.

EMAILS (global): only 3 official addresses - info@, partnership@, press@dailytechwire.com - wired everywhere by context. (Domain shown as .com; all-context.md references .asia, flag the inconsistency.)
COVER-ART LABELS: all text label overlays/figcaptions on the geometric cover-art images (DTW HERO, FEATURED, EIC PORTRAIT, etc.) removed globally; only the abstract composition shows.
invariantWatch: INVARIANT #11 VIOLATED by final design: the design REINTRODUCES a header logo - a navy 'DTW' monogram block + lowercase 'dailytechwire' wordmark + pulse line with a terracotta/amber dot. Invariant #11 says NO logo badge in the header (wordmark + tagline only). The design bundle's dtw-logo-primary.svg matches this reintroduced mark. Must be flagged before building the production header.

INVARIANT #7 CONFLICT: brand accent in the final design is terracotta #D4623C, but invariant #7 fixes the coral accent at #E04E1F. The design also introduces a soft navy --banner token (#1B2A52 / #16223C) not listed in invariant #7's fixed colors. Reconcile which palette governs production.

INVARIANT #4: paywall sign-in nudge fires after 3+ reads (correct, soft block, in-flow, dismissible) BUT the '3' is hardcoded in the prototype; invariant #4 requires it be CMS-configurable, never hardcoded. Production must read the threshold from CMS.
INVARIANT #5: GOOD - article detail removed AI-assisted badge + AI disclosure boxes + affiliate disclosure; sponsored disclosure kept. Do NOT reintroduce AI badges or kind='ai' boxes when porting.

INVARIANT #12: footer compliance line dropped 'Nghi dinh 13 (VN)' on user instruction (now 'GDPR - PDPA (SG)' only), but invariant #12 still lists Nghi dinh 13 (Vietnam) as a data-residency obligation. Reconcile legal scope vs. footer display copy.

INVARIANT #13: GOOD - Awards page matches (no medallion/categories/winners/dates, restrained 'Coming this year' / 'Launching next year' hero). Note copy drifted to 'next year' since awards now launch later; keep year references generic.
DOMAIN INCONSISTENCY: design uses @dailytechwire.com emails; all-context.md references dailytechwire.asia (tips@dailytechwire.asia). Pick one canonical domain.

SPONSORED STRIP (homepage) and dashboards sponsor slot were hidden/removed as a Phase-1 presentation choice; this does not change the mandatory non-dismissable sponsored disclosure boxes on sponsored ARTICLES (invariants #5/#6); keep that logic intact when these surfaces are re-enabled.

decisions.topic: Dark-mode text contrast fix (var(--paper) on var(--banner))
decisions.summary: chat2: User reported many texts unreadable in dark mode, especially About and Newsroom. Root cause found: text using color:var(--paper) placed on a var(--banner) navy background. Banner stays dark in both themes but --paper flips to dark (#0F172A) in dark mode -> dark-on-dark invisible text. Fixed only the banner-context cases to white: About/Newsroom hero intro emphasis, the 5 stat numbers, the 'Securely contact the newsroom' heading; and homepage 'Asia Tech Spotlight' article titles. Cases of var(--paper) on var(--ink) (badges, play buttons, primary button) were deliberately left untouched because both flip and stay readable inverted.
decisions.finalState: FIXED & verified (verifier: No issues found)
affectedSurfaces: about.jsx (hero emphasis, stat numbers, secure-contact heading)

decisions.topic: Global contrast / saturation reduction (calm editorial palette)
decisions.summary: chat2: User asked to tone down contrast/saturation across ALL pages to match the already-softened homepage+header. Audit found loud hardcoded hex only in about.jsx (BEATS dots + REVENUE mix bar/legend) and art.jsx (Avatar palette + CITY_COLOR map). Mapped loud colors to muted editorial palette: coral #E04E1F->#B0512E, sky #0EA5E9->teal #3E6E80, purple #7C3AED->indigo #3A4E8C, green #16A34A->#46735C, amber #D97706->olive #8F7238. ART_PALETTES cover-art blocks left untouched (they are the approved homepage reference). Dashboards/pillar/newsletters already use the muted pillar CSS vars.
decisions.finalState: DONE & verified. NOTE: introduces new muted hex values not in invariant-7 brand list.
affectedSurfaces: art.jsx (Avatar palette, CITY_COLOR — shared across pages)

decisions.topic: About vs Newsroom page differentiation + remove Ownership & funding
decisions.summary: chat2: Removed the 'Ownership & funding' section entirely from both pages. Split the two pages by intent via a variant prop: /about = TRUST focus (hero 'Independent journalism…', 'Who we are', 'Mission & values', editorial-standards 'Read the rules' links — independence/principles/transparency). /about/newsroom = DEPTH focus (hero 'Inside the newsroom', 'What we cover' 8-beat grid, Editor-in-Chief profile, full Masthead, 'Six bureaus' grid with city/role/chief). Both share the secure-contact block and business-info footer. Bureaus stat cards restructured to stack cleanly (avoid name/label collision).
decisions.finalState: DONE & verified

compare.designChangeSummary:
The updated homepage.jsx is a tracked, modified file (clear diff available). The net-new design intent (beyond cosmetic weight/size tweaks) is:

1. NEW NAVY BANNER TOKENS. Two new semantic CSS vars are introduced in design/project/index.html and consumed by homepage.jsx: --banner (#1B2A52 light / #16223C dark) and --brand-navy (#1B2A52 light / #E2E8F0 dark). The two big dark banners — AsiaSpotlight and NewsletterCTA — switch their background from var(--ink) to var(--banner), and their text/hairlines move from var(--paper)/color-mix(var(--paper)) to hard-coded #FFFFFF / #E8EDF7 / rgba(232,237,247,...). SectionHeader's 2px bottom rule moves from var(--ink) to var(--brand-navy). Intent: softer deep-navy banners and section rules instead of pure black, with the navy lightening correctly in dark mode.

2. AWARDS MEDALLION REMOVED. The 76px circular "EST / 2026" medallion is deleted; the banner grid goes from `auto 1fr auto` to `1fr auto`; copy changes "The inaugural awards arrive in 2026" -> "The inaugural awards launch next year" (and the body line "...revealed later this year" -> "...revealed closer to launch"); the CTA Button variant goes primary -> accent. This aligns the homepage banner with awards year-one invariant (no medallion, no year-specific claim).

3. NEWSLETTER COUNT 6 -> 8. Kicker "Six newsletters" -> "Eight newsletters"; the list adds "Deep Dive, DTW Awards" after "Products & Deals" in all three locales.

4. SPONSORED STRIP HIDDEN. <SponsoredStrip/> in the Homepage composition is commented out ("Paid Partner / Sponsored strip temporarily hidden").

5. ASIA SPOTLIGHT CTA RETARGETED. Button changes from navigate("/asia") "See all Asia coverage →" to navigate("/latest") "See all latest →"; the heading loses its trailing period in all three locales.

6. Cosmetic: hero title 46/700 -> 44/650; section/spotlight/deepdive/awards heading weights 700 -> 650; pillar-showcase pillar label weight 700 -> 650; Homepage container gains inline padding "0 24px 10px"; small punctuation cleanups in mock copy (em-dash -> comma) which are mock-data and not gaps.
compare.currentProductionSummary:
Production fully mirrors the homepage section structure: apps/web/src/app/(reader)/page.tsx is an RSC that fetches recent articles, deep dive, sponsored article, and wire drops from Payload and composes HomeHero, BriefBand, WireDrops, PillarShowcase, AsiaSpotlight, DashboardsTeaser, DeepDive, AwardsBanner, SponsoredStrip, BestOfReviews, PodcastStrip, NewsletterCta inside Reveal wrappers. Components live in apps/web/src/components/home/ as Tailwind + CSS-var TSX reading real Payload data, with responsive helper classes (.r-hero, .r-awards, etc.) in globals.css.

Relative to the new design, production is on the PREVIOUS version:
- AsiaSpotlight (asia-spotlight.tsx) background is var(--ink), text var(--paper) / color-mix(var(--paper)), CTA href="/asia" "See all Asia coverage →", heading ends with a period.
- NewsletterCta (newsletter-cta.tsx) background is var(--ink), kicker "Six newsletters", list ends at "Products & Deals" (6 items), borders/inputs use color-mix(var(--paper)...).
- SectionHeader (section-header.tsx) bottom rule is `2px solid var(--ink)`, title weight 700.
- AwardsBanner (awards-banner.tsx) STILL renders the 76px "EST 2026" medallion, uses `.r-awards` (auto 1fr auto) grid, copy "The inaugural awards arrive in 2026", CTA variant="primary".
- SponsoredStrip is STILL composed in page.tsx (line 82-84).
- HomeHero title uses clamp(28px,6vw,46px)/700; container has no extra inline padding.
- globals.css does NOT define --banner or --brand-navy in either :root or the dark block.
- Production --accent is #E04E1F (correct per invariant #7); the design index.html's softened #D4623C is intentionally NOT adopted.

gaps.description: Missing CSS tokens: globals.css does not define --banner or --brand-navy (nor their dark-mode values). Any component referencing them would fall back to invalid/transparent. These must be added before the banner/section-header restyle can land.

AwardsBanner still renders the 76px 'EST 2026' circular medallion and uses the 3-column `auto 1fr auto` grid. The new design removed the medallion (2-col `1fr auto`). This is also a year-one Awards invariant concern (#13: no medallion).

AsiaSpotlight background uses var(--ink) instead of the new var(--banner); design wants the softer navy banner with #FFFFFF/#E8EDF7 text. Currently renders as pure black (light) / light-paper (dark) rather than navy.

NewsletterCta background uses var(--ink) instead of var(--banner); also still says 'Six newsletters' and lists only 6 newsletters. Design wants 'Eight newsletters' and adds 'Deep Dive, DTW Awards' to the list (all three locales).
SectionHeader bottom rule is var(--ink); design moved it to var(--brand-navy). Affects every band header on the homepage (WireDrops, PillarShowcase, DashboardsTeaser, DeepDive, etc.).
AsiaSpotlight CTA points to /asia with label 'See all Asia coverage'; design retargets to /latest with 'See all latest'. Also heading trailing period removed in design.
AwardsBanner copy 'arrive in 2026' vs design 'launch next year' (year-specific claim removed); CTA variant primary vs design accent.
Heading font-weights are 700 across SectionHeader/AsiaSpotlight/DeepDive/AwardsBanner/NewsletterCta/PillarShowcase and hero is 46/700; design softened all to ~650 and hero to 44. Cosmetic-only.
SponsoredStrip is still composed in page.tsx; design commented it out ('temporarily hidden'). Likely a product/business toggle rather than a hard removal — confirm intent before removing.

recommendedUpdates.change: Add --banner and --brand-navy tokens to both :root and html[data-theme="dark"] in globals.css, mirroring the design (light: --banner #1B2A52, --brand-navy #1B2A52; dark: --banner #16223C, --brand-navy #E2E8F0). Update process/context/uxui/all-uxui.md token list to match. Do NOT adopt the design's softened accent #D4623C — keep --accent #E04E1F per invariant #7.

Restyle AsiaSpotlight: background var(--ink) -> var(--banner); heading/label text -> #FFFFFF; meta text -> rgba(232,237,247,0.60); retarget CTA href '/asia' -> '/latest' and label 'See all Asia coverage →' -> 'See all latest →' (all 3 locales); drop heading trailing period; soften heading weight 700 -> 650.

Restyle NewsletterCta: background var(--ink) -> var(--banner); heading -> #FFFFFF, body -> rgba(232,237,247,0.70), input border/background and ghost button border -> rgba(232,237,247,0.20)/0.06; update kicker 'Six newsletters' -> 'Eight newsletters' and append 'Deep Dive, DTW Awards' to the newsletter list in all 3 locales; weight 700 -> 650. Note: real subscribe wiring (Resend double opt-in) is a separate task; the demo alert() stays out of production regardless.
Update SectionHeader bottom rule borderBottom from '2px solid var(--ink)' to '2px solid var(--brand-navy)' and title weight 700 -> 650. Shared component — visually affects all homepage band headers.

Remove the 76px 'EST 2026' medallion block from AwardsBanner; change grid to 2-col (drop .r-awards `auto 1fr auto`, use `1fr auto` and update the .r-awards / mobile rule in globals.css accordingly); update copy 'The inaugural awards arrive in 2026' -> 'The inaugural awards launch next year' and body 'revealed later this year' -> 'revealed closer to launch' (all 3 locales); CTA variant primary -> accent; heading weight 700 -> 650. Reconciles the homepage banner with Awards year-one invariant #13.

Soften remaining heading weights 700 -> 650 to match design: HomeHero hero title (and size clamp max 46 -> 44), DeepDive h3, PillarShowcase pillar label. Optionally add the homepage container inline padding '0 24px 10px' if not already provided by .container. Cosmetic.

Confirm intent on SponsoredStrip: the design comments it out as 'temporarily hidden'. If this is a product decision, gate it (e.g., only render when a sponsored article exists / a CMS flag is on) rather than hard-deleting, so it can be re-enabled without code change. Do not remove from page.tsx until confirmed.

invariantConflicts: Invariant #13 (Awards year-one: no medallion): production apps/web/src/components/home/awards-banner.tsx renders a 'EST 2026' medallion. The new design removes it, which BRINGS production into compliance — so adopting the design fixes the conflict. Flagging because the current production state conflicts with #13 and the awards detail page should be checked for the same medallion.

Invariant #7 (brand colors fixed; coral accent #E04E1F): the design's index.html softens --accent to #D4623C. Production correctly keeps #E04E1F. Do NOT port the design's accent value when adding the new --banner/--brand-navy tokens — that would violate #7. (No conflict in production today; this is a guardrail for the implementation.)
openQuestions: Is hiding the Sponsored / Paid Partner strip a permanent product decision or just 'temporarily hidden' (the design comment)? This affects whether to delete vs feature-gate SponsoredStrip in page.tsx, and relates to the editorial sponsored-content surface.

The new --banner token is a softer navy (#1B2A52). Should it be a true design-system token added to the Tailwind @theme map in globals.css (so utilities like bg-banner exist), or only a raw CSS var consumed via inline style? Pick one for consistency with how --paper/--ink are mapped.

Newsletter list now claims 'Eight newsletters' including 'Deep Dive' and 'DTW Awards', but the newsletters feature spec lists 6 (AM/PM Brief, AI Weekly, Asia Funding Weekly, Dev Digest, Products Deals). Confirm the canonical newsletter count before updating copy, since the /newsletters page and Resend segments must agree.
Should the AsiaSpotlight CTA truly point at /latest instead of /asia? Confirm a /latest route exists in production (only /[pillar] dynamic routes were observed).

compare.designChangeSummary: The article.jsx design file was MODIFIED (tracked; git diff is non-empty), not new. The substantive design changes (ignoring whitespace/formatting reflows) are: (1) AI-ASSISTED disclosure fully removed — both the header `AI-ASSISTED` mono badge and the three `kind="ai"` DisclosureBoxes (top/middle/bottom) are gone; comments now read `{/* AI disclosure removed */}`. (2) The affiliate disclosure block (the `$`-badge box with "Affiliate disclosure: Some links...") is fully removed, replaced by `{/* Affiliate disclosure removed */}`. (3) i18n was layered in throughout: `useLang()`, `localizedPillarLabel(article.pillar, lang)` for the breadcrumb, `fmtDateL(article.published, lang)` for the date, and `t(en, vi, id)` translations for the DEEP DIVE badge, PAID PARTNER badge, "min read", the hero caption, the AudioPlayerBar title ("Listen to this article"), RelatedRow "Read next", and the corrections box. (4) RelatedRow top border changed from `var(--ink)` to `var(--brand-navy)`. (5) Copy changes: paywall H3 changed from "Keep reading the reporting that matters in Asia tech." to "Keep reading the reporting that matters in tech, across Asia and the world"; corrections email changed from `corrections@dtw.news` to `editor@dailytechwire.com`; en-dashes normalized to commas in body/paywall copy. (6) Minor weights: body h3 fontWeight 700->650, paywall H3 700->650.
compare.currentProductionSummary: Production is a real RSC/Tailwind split: page.tsx fetches from Payload (draft-aware) and passes ArticleView + Lexical body to the client `ArticleContent`. `ArticleContent` renders breadcrumb, header (pillar tag, section, DEEP DIVE/AI-ASSISTED/PAID PARTNER badges), responsive hero (real `heroImageUrl` img with `clamp()` fallback to CoverArt), AudioPlayerBar, ArticleBody, ShareBar/Paywall, affiliate disclosure, RelatedRow, and corrections box. `ArticleBody` renders Payload Lexical RichText, splitting root children in half to inject middle disclosure boxes, with TopBoxes/BottomBoxes wrappers. Production already has i18n wired in several places the design also localizes: breadcrumb pillar label, header pillar tag label, date via `fmtDateL`, and "min read" via `t()`. Paywall, RelatedRow border (`var(--ink)`), corrections email (`corrections@dailytechwire.asia`), and several badges/labels are still hardcoded English. CRITICALLY, production STILL renders the AI-ASSISTED header badge (article-content.tsx L82-97), the `kind="ai"` DisclosureBoxes at top/middle/bottom (article-body.tsx L37, L48, L88), and the affiliate disclosure block (article-content.tsx L205-240) — all of which the updated design has removed.

gaps.description: INVARIANT #5 VIOLATION already live in production: the AI-ASSISTED inline disclosure was removed by product decision 2026-06-05, but production still renders the `AI-ASSISTED` header badge (article-content.tsx L82-97) and the three `kind="ai"` DisclosureBoxes top/middle/bottom (article-body.tsx L37, L48, L88, plus the disclosed/TopBoxes/BottomBoxes plumbing). The updated design has correctly removed all of these. Production must be brought in line.

Affiliate disclosure block divergence: the design removed the in-article affiliate disclosure box, but production still renders it (article-content.tsx L205-240, gated on `article.affiliate`). This is a deliberate-looking design removal, not a mock-data artifact, so it is a real intent gap. NOTE: this is a policy/editorial-integrity decision, not a coded invariant — confirm before deleting, since affiliate-link disclosure is part of the publication's transparency posture.

i18n gaps: production hardcodes English for the DEEP DIVE header badge (article-content.tsx L79), the PAID PARTNER badge (L112), the corrections box ("Spot something wrong? Email ... We log every correction publicly." L256-257), the AudioPlayerBar title "Listen to this article" (audio-player.tsx L51), the hero caption, and ShareBar button labels. The design wraps the first four in `t(en, vi, id)`. Per invariant #10 these are chrome strings (not body), so they should be localized.
RelatedRow top border color: design changed to `var(--brand-navy)`; production still uses `borderTop: "3px solid var(--ink)"` (related-row.tsx L31). Purely visual.

Paywall headline copy out of date: production says "Keep reading the reporting that matters in Asia tech." (paywall.tsx L51); design updated to "Keep reading the reporting that matters in tech, across Asia and the world". Copy-only.

Corrections email mismatch: production uses `corrections@dailytechwire.asia` (article-content.tsx L257); design uses `editor@dailytechwire.com`. Both differ from each other and from earlier `corrections@dtw.news`. Needs a single source-of-truth decision rather than blindly copying the design (the design value may itself be a placeholder).

Minor heading weight drift: design lowered body `<h3>` weight 700->650 and paywall H3 700->650 (a softer editorial weight). Production uses 700 in paywall.tsx L48 and the Lexical RichText headings are styled elsewhere. Cosmetic.

recommendedUpdates.change: Remove the AI-ASSISTED header badge entirely (the `{article.aiAssisted && (AI-ASSISTED)}` block). The `aiAssisted` field stays on ArticleView/Payload per invariant #5 — only stop surfacing it inline. Leave the DEEP DIVE and PAID PARTNER badges intact.

Remove all `kind="ai"` DisclosureBoxes from the article body: the top box in TopBoxes (L37), the bottom box in BottomBoxes (L48), and the middle box (L88). Simplify the `disclosed` gate to depend only on `article.sponsored` so the middle wrapper only fires for sponsored. Keep all sponsored top/middle/bottom boxes and the body-splitting logic untouched (invariant #5 sponsored boxes must remain non-dismissable at top+middle+bottom).

Remove the affiliate disclosure block (the `{article.affiliate && (...)}` box). CONFIRM with product first — this is an editorial-transparency surface, not a coded invariant; the design removal may be intentional or may be deferred to the Best-of-Reviews affiliate flow. If kept, at minimum localize its copy.

Localize remaining hardcoded chrome strings with the existing `t(en, vi, id)` helper to match the design: DEEP DIVE badge, PAID PARTNER badge, and the corrections box in article-content.tsx; "Listen to this article" in audio-player.tsx; and (optional) ShareBar labels in share-bar.tsx. Use the design's exact vi/id strings as the source. Note audio-player.tsx and share-bar.tsx would need `useT` added (they are already client components).

Change RelatedRow top border from `var(--ink)` to `var(--brand-navy)` to match the design, and update the "Read next" id translation (design id uses "Baca berikutnya"; production uses "Baca selanjutnya" — pick one).
Update the paywall headline copy to the new design string and consider lowering the H3 fontWeight 700->650 to match. Copy-only; verify the new line against the canonical spec before applying.

Reconcile the corrections email to a single canonical value (design says editor@dailytechwire.com, prod says corrections@dailytechwire.asia). Do not blindly copy the design — confirm the real inbox/domain, then localize the surrounding sentence with t().

invariantConflicts: Invariant #5: the updated design CORRECTLY removes the AI-assisted inline disclosure (header AI-ASSISTED badge + the 3 kind="ai" DisclosureBoxes). Production currently STILL renders them (article-content.tsx L82-97 header badge; article-body.tsx L37/L48/L88 ai boxes), which means production is presently OUT OF COMPLIANCE with invariant #5. Aligning to the design resolves the violation. The `aiAssisted` field must remain on the Article schema/ArticleView — only the inline surfacing is removed.

Invariant #5 (sponsored side): the design KEEPS all three sponsored DisclosureBoxes (top/middle/bottom) and they must stay non-dismissable. Recommended edits preserve them; do not let the ai-box removal accidentally drop the sponsored middle box (the shared `disclosed` gate currently couples them).

Invariant #11 / #13: not triggered by this surface. The design's new dtw-logo-primary.svg does not appear in article.jsx, and there is no header logo or awards content here — no conflict to flag on the article surface.
openQuestions: Affiliate disclosure: is its removal from the article body an intentional policy change, or just deferred to the Best-of-Reviews/affiliate-link flow? The about-trust Sponsored/Affiliate Policy and editorial-integrity posture imply affiliate links still need disclosure somewhere — confirm before deleting the block.
Corrections email: which address is canonical — editor@dailytechwire.com (design), corrections@dailytechwire.asia (current prod), or another? Domain (.com vs .asia) also needs confirming.

Paywall threshold: the design and production both hardcode `articlesRead > 3` and the copy "your 3 free articles". Invariant #4 requires the meter limit to be CMS-configurable and never hardcoded. This predates this design change but should be tracked — should the article surface read the threshold from CMS config now, or is that still deferred to the Phase 2 paywall work?

Audio bar: the design still ships the AudioPlayerBar (TTS is a Phase 2 feature per context). Confirm it should remain rendered as a demo/placeholder vs gated behind a feature flag — out of scope for this design diff but worth noting.
Should ShareBar labels (Save/Share/Copy link/Email) be localized too? The design left them in English; only the header/audio/corrections chrome was wrapped in t().

compare.designChangeSummary: The pillar.jsx diff is mostly an i18n retrofit (already largely implemented in production) plus a small set of intentional changes: (1) the `asia` pillar was renamed to `latest` and given NEW semantics — a cross-pillar auto-aggregated feed (every story across all pillars, newest first, no manual curation) instead of a normal pillar with its own articles; (2) heading/kicker/featured font-weight dropped 700 -> 650 (a bundle-wide weight softening); (3) the "Load more" button gained explicit larger sizing (padding 18x36, fontSize 15, letterSpacing .02em); (4) the end-of-feed copy now includes the story count ("End of feed, N stories shown.") instead of a bare message; (5) headings/descriptions for `latest` added to the localized pillarHeading/pillarDescription tables. The page still accepts a `subsection` prop for deep-linking into a specific subsection tab. Mock-data extras (the static "6 reporters · updated 12 min ago" suffix) are not real intent.
compare.currentProductionSummary: Production (page.tsx + pillar-content.tsx) already renders the full pillar layout: CMS-driven header (icon, DTW · label kicker, H1 heading, description, Follow/RSS buttons, story count), horizontally-scrollable subsection tabs with a Sort select, a 1.3fr/1fr featured article, a responsive 3-up grid (auto-fit minmax instead of fixed repeat(3)), a Load-more paginator (PAGE_SIZE=21), and empty/end-of-feed states. i18n via useT/useLang/localizedPillarLabel is fully wired (matching the design's i18n changes). Pillar identity/heading/description/color/icon all come from Payload (getPillars + getArticlesByPillar), honoring invariant #8 (CMS-driven routes). Production already improved on the design with responsive clamp() font sizes and a fluid grid. Differences from the new design: PillarId/PILLARS still use `asia` (not `latest`) and there is no aggregated "latest" feed; kicker/H1/featured weights are still 700; Load-more uses the default size="lg" with no enlarged padding; end-of-feed copy is the bare "End of feed." without a count; the `subsection` deep-link is not routable (no subsection route segment).

gaps.description: Design renamed the `asia` pillar to `latest` with new cross-pillar aggregation semantics (all articles, newest first, no curation). Production still has `asia` as a normal pillar and has no `latest` feed. Because pillars are CMS entities, the existing `/[pillar]/page.tsx` would 404 on `latest` (no matching pillar doc) and `getArticlesByPillar` only queries a single pillar — it cannot aggregate across all pillars.
Kicker, H1, and featured H2 font-weight is 700 in production but the design moved to 650 (a softer weight applied bundle-wide). Purely visual.
Load-more button lost the design's enlarged styling (padding 18x36, fontSize 15, letterSpacing .02em). Production uses the plain size="lg" button, which is visually smaller/tighter than intended.

End-of-feed message: design wants the story count inline ("End of feed, N stories shown."); production shows a bare "End of feed.". The count exists client-side (visible.length) so it is safe to compute without hydration mismatch.

The `subsection` deep-link the design page accepts (to preselect a subsection tab via URL) has no production route segment; subsection state is in-memory only and not shareable/linkable. Pre-existing limitation, reaffirmed by the design.

Localized heading/description tables for the `latest` feed were added in the design but cannot land until a `latest` surface exists. If `latest` is implemented as a non-CMS route, these strings need a home (currently pillar copy comes from the CMS pillar doc, not a static table).

recommendedUpdates.change: Decide and implement the `asia` -> `latest` direction. Recommended: keep `asia` as a real CMS pillar if still desired, and add a SEPARATE aggregated `latest` feed as a non-CMS route (e.g. its own /latest segment or a special-cased branch in [pillar]/page.tsx) that calls getRecentArticles across all pillars instead of getArticlesByPillar. Reuse PillarContent by passing a static heading/description/color/icon for the latest feed. Do NOT silently delete the `asia` pillar — that is a CMS/data decision (invariant #8) and also affects the homepage Asia Spotlight, cover-art, icons, and nav. Confirm product intent first.
Soften kicker/H1/featured-H2 fontWeight from 700 to 650 to match the design's bundle-wide weight change.
Apply the design's enlarged Load-more button styling (style={{ padding: '18px 36px', fontSize: 15, letterSpacing: '.02em' }}) on the existing outline size="lg" button.

Include the shown-story count in the end-of-feed message, matching the design ("End of feed, {visible.length} stories shown.") with vi/id translations via useT. Computed client-side so no hydration risk.

Optional: support a routable subsection so tabs are deep-linkable (e.g. accept a subsection segment and pass an initial activeSub into PillarContent). Defer unless product wants shareable subsection URLs.

invariantConflicts: Invariant #8 (Pillar/Sub-section/Tag are CMS entities; routes regenerate from CMS): The design's `asia`->`latest` rename and the `latest` aggregated feed are NOT a code-only change. `asia` is (or should be) a CMS pillar, so renaming/removing it is a CMS data operation, and `latest` is a synthetic cross-pillar feed that is NOT a CMS pillar — it must be implemented as a special non-CMS route rather than expecting a pillar doc with slug `latest`. Do not hardcode a replacement pillar list in code; coordinate the CMS change and the aggregated-feed route together.
openQuestions: Is `asia` being fully replaced by `latest`, or does `latest` coexist as an additional aggregated feed while `asia` remains a pillar? This changes whether it is a CMS rename + homepage Asia Spotlight rework, or an additive new route.

Should the `latest` aggregated feed live at /latest (its own route) or be special-cased inside the [pillar] dynamic route? The latter keeps one component but risks colliding with a future CMS pillar slugged `latest`.
Does the new `latest` feed need its own heading/description copy source (static i18n table vs a CMS singleton), given pillar copy currently comes from the Payload pillar doc?
Should subsection tabs become deep-linkable URLs now, or stay in-memory? The design page signature keeps accepting a `subsection` param but no production route provides it.

compare.designChangeSummary: The dashboards.jsx design file IS tracked (committed in initial commit f6b3076), so `git diff` shows concrete deltas vs the prior version. The new design intent has six changes: (1) HEADER BORDER recolor from `var(--ink)` to a new token `var(--brand-navy)` (#1B2A52 light / #E2E8F0 dark, defined in design index.html). (2) i18n: every chrome string (kicker, H1, lede, tab labels, section H2s, sub-meta, country label, chart captions, AI 'Optimize for' label + sort-pill labels) is now wrapped in a `useT()` `t(en, vi, id)` call. (3) Heading font-weight softened 700 -> 650 on the H1 and both section H2s. (4) The kicker reads 'Data Desk · Live' with a live-dot, and the lede claims 'Updated every 15 minutes' (live-feed framing). (5) The funding sub-meta is a live status line: 'Last update 12:42 SGT · next in 14 min · 138 instruments tracked' with a live-dot; the AI sub-meta is 'Sort by what you actually use the model for · 18 models tracked · methodology disclosed'. (6) BigChart drops the bottom gridline (now `[0.25,.5,.75,1]` instead of `[0,.25,.5,.75,1]`). MOST SIGNIFICANT: the entire 'Methodology + sponsor slot' two-column section was DELETED from DashboardsLanding in this design iteration.
compare.currentProductionSummary: Production renders a working dashboards page at /dashboards/[[...sub]] with funding|ai tabs (Link-based, server-routable). It is intentionally framed as a PREVIEW / coming-soon, not live: kicker reads 'Data Desk · Preview' (no live-dot), H1 is 'Dashboards' (not 'Live Dashboards'), and the lede says 'figures shown are sample data while we wire up the feeds. Coming soon.' Both trackers (FundingTracker, AILeaderboard) are fully built client components with sortable headers, country segmented filter, CSV button, BigChart SVG, top-movers list, and AI bar visualizations — matching the design's table/chart structure closely. Sub-meta lines are preview-framed: 'Sample data — live market feed coming soon' and 'sample data, preview' instead of the design's live-status strings. Production STILL renders the Methodology + Sponsor-slot two-column section that the new design removed. H1 uses responsive `clamp(30px,8vw,48px)`; section H2s and H1 use fontWeight 700. Header border is `var(--ink)`. No i18n: all strings are hardcoded English even though a matching `useT()` hook exists in lib/i18n.tsx. Production adds responsive helpers absent from design (`r-split-21`, `r-table-scroll` for mobile horizontal scroll).

gaps.description: i18n not wired: production hardcodes every chrome string in English. The design now localizes all dashboard chrome (tabs, headings, captions, status lines, filter labels) via useT(). A matching useT() hook already exists at apps/web/src/lib/i18n.tsx with the exact (en,vi,id) signature, but none of the dashboard components are client-localized. This conflicts with invariant #9 (i18n year-1 en/id/vi) and #10 (chrome translated). Note the three dashboard components are already 'use client', so calling useT() is straightforward.

Methodology + Sponsor-slot section divergence: the new design DELETED this two-column block from the landing, but production still renders it. This is a genuine product-direction question, not a clear bug. The dashboards feature scope (per all-context.md) explicitly lists 'methodology, sponsor slot' as required, and the SponsorSlots Payload collection exists. Removing it in production would drop the only sponsor surface on this page and the 'not investment/procurement advice' disclaimer. Do NOT silently delete — confirm whether the design intends permanent removal or relocation.

Live-vs-Preview framing mismatch: design frames the page as LIVE (kicker 'Data Desk · Live' + live-dot, 'Updated every 15 minutes', live status meta with 'next in 14 min'). Production deliberately frames it as a PREVIEW with sample data ('Coming soon'). Since both trackers run on mock data from lib/data.ts (no live feed wired), production's honest 'preview/sample' framing is arguably correct until real feeds land; adopting the design's live copy now would over-promise. This is a copy/status decision to confirm, not a pure styling gap.

Header border color: design switched to var(--brand-navy); production uses var(--ink). The --brand-navy token (#1B2A52 light / #E2E8F0 dark) is NOT defined in apps/web/src/app/globals.css — adopting it requires adding the token first, or the border will fall back to invalid/empty.
Heading font-weight: design softened H1 + section H2s from 700 to 650; production still uses 700.
BigChart baseline gridline: design removed the bottom (p=0) gridline; production still renders 5 lines including the baseline at p=0.

AI Leaderboard Bar max divergence: design Bar divides by an explicit `max=100` prop (`(v/max)*100`); production hardcodes `width: ${v}%` (implicitly assuming a 0-100 scale). Functionally identical only while all scores are <=100; if any benchmark score exceeds 100 the production bar overflows its track. Minor robustness gap, not a visual gap today.

recommendedUpdates.change: Wire i18n into the three dashboard components and the landing page: convert the page header/tab labels and the FundingTracker/AILeaderboard chrome strings to useT() t(en,vi,id) calls, mirroring the design's exact strings. Hook already exists; components are already client. Keep table cell DATA untranslated (numbers/tickers/model names are not chrome).

Decide and apply the Methodology+Sponsor section direction. RECOMMENDED: do NOT blindly delete to match the prototype. Confirm with product whether the section is permanently dropped or relocated; if kept, leave the production block (it satisfies the documented dashboards scope + sponsor surface + disclaimer). If removal is confirmed, remove the section from page.tsx and verify the SponsorSlots collection has another consumer so the dashboard sponsor placement is not orphaned.

Hold the live-vs-preview copy as-is until real feeds are wired. Keep production's 'Data Desk · Preview' / 'Coming soon' / 'sample data' framing while data.ts is mock. When live feeds land, switch to the design's 'Data Desk · Live' kicker + live-dot, 'Live Dashboards' H1, and the live status meta lines (also localize them via useT at that time).

Add the --brand-navy token (light #1B2A52, dark #E2E8F0) to globals.css :root and the dark-theme block, then switch the dashboards header border from var(--ink) to var(--brand-navy). Add the token first so the border does not fall back to empty. Coordinate with other surfaces that may also adopt this token.
Soften heading weights from 700 to 650 on the page H1 and both section H2s to match the design.
Remove the baseline (p=0) gridline in BigChart to match the design's `[0.25,0.5,0.75,1]` array. Optional polish.
Optional robustness: pass an explicit max to the AILeaderboard Bar (matching the design's max=100 contract) so a future >100 score does not overflow the bar track.

invariantConflicts: Invariant #9 (i18n year-1 en/id/vi subpath routing): the dashboards surface is fully hardcoded English while the design now localizes all chrome via useT(). Production is out of compliance until i18n is wired.

Invariant #10 (chrome translated, body stays source language): dashboard chrome (tabs, headings, status lines, filter labels) is chrome and must be translatable; currently it is not. Table data (tickers, model names, numbers) is correctly NOT chrome and should remain untranslated.

Invariant #7 (brand colors fixed): no violation, but note the new design introduces a --brand-navy token (#1B2A52 / dark #E2E8F0) that is not in the documented fixed-color list and is undefined in production globals.css; adding it is a token addition, not a conflict, but should be recorded.
openQuestions: Is the Methodology + Sponsor-slot section permanently removed (matching the new prototype), or just relocated? The dashboards feature scope in all-context.md still lists 'methodology, sponsor slot' as required, and a SponsorSlots Payload collection exists — confirm before deleting from production.

Should the dashboards page flip from 'Preview / sample data / Coming soon' to the design's 'Live' framing now, or stay preview-framed until real market/AI feeds replace lib/data.ts mock data? Adopting live copy now would over-promise.
Should --brand-navy be added as a shared global token (used by multiple surfaces) or scoped locally to the dashboards header? Other design surfaces may reference the same token.

The design's live status strings include hardcoded values ('12:42 SGT', 'next in 14 min', '138 instruments', '18 models tracked') — when feeds go live, should these be CMS/feed-driven rather than literal, and how does that interact with i18n string wrapping?

compare.designChangeSummary: The trust.jsx design changed in three substantive ways vs its previous version: (1) ALL trust copy is now internationalised with the t(en, vi, id) inline helper across every page (editorial, ai, corrections, sponsored) plus the nav label, "was/now" correction labels, and section headings — Year-1 en/vi/id; (2) the `transparency` page was REMOVED from the trust slug map entirely (its five mock sections — Headcount, Revenue mix, Removed posts, Government requests, Newsletter deliverability — are deleted, so the left-nav now lists only Editorial / AI / Corrections / Sponsored); (3) several copy edits: editorial "How we report" broadened from "across Asia tech" to "across tech, in Asia and worldwide"; Independence drops "Pro" ("Pro membership revenue" -> "membership revenue"); the Corrections section text drops the inline "See /trust/corrections." pointer; the Sponsored/DTW Studio section drops the sentence "Writers, editors, and the EIC of DTW Studio do not work on the newsroom."; Affiliate drops "Q4" ("Q4 transparency report" -> "transparency report"); and the en-dash separator in StaticPage became a comma. The h1 fontWeight was softened 700 -> 650. Layout (220px 1fr sticky sidebar, 720px article column, 48px serif title, was/now grid with up/down colors) is otherwise unchanged.
compare.currentProductionSummary: Production is a single async Server Component at app/(reader)/trust/[slug]/page.tsx that renders all trust pages from a hardcoded PAGES map keyed by slug. It faithfully reproduces the design's visual structure and improves on it in several ways: the two-column 220px 1fr grid is implemented via the responsive `.r-sidebar` class that stacks to a single column under 768px (the design has no responsive handling); the h1 uses fluid `clamp(30px, 8vw, 48px)`; and the Corrections page is wired to REAL Payload data through getCorrections() (mapping correctionDate/wasText/nowText/editor/summary), with a graceful empty state and an editor "Signed off by" line — both beyond the design mock. However, production has NO i18n: all copy is English-only literal strings, and it cannot trivially adopt useT() because that hook is client-only ("use client" in lib/i18n.tsx) while this page must stay async to fetch corrections from Payload. Production STILL ships the full `transparency` page with invented specifics (27 reporters/6 editors, a 62/22/12/4 revenue mix, "Singapore: 0. India: 1. Vietnam: 0" government requests, 98.7% Gmail deliverability) and STILL lists it in the trust slug union and the left-nav. The about page (TRUST_LINKS + an inline body Link) and footer.tsx both still link to /trust/transparency. Production also retains the older copy on every delta point the design changed (Asia-tech scope, "Pro membership revenue", "See /trust/corrections.", the DTW-Studio-EIC sentence, "Q4 transparency report", h1 weight 700).

gaps.description: No i18n on the trust pages. The new design internationalises every string with t(en, vi, id), but production renders English-only. This is the largest gap and is architecturally awkward: the trust page is an async Server Component (it awaits getCorrections() from Payload), whereas the production useT() in lib/i18n.tsx is a client-only hook backed by a localStorage-hydrated context. Matching the design requires either splitting the page into a server data-loader + client presentational component, or building a server-readable language source. Note: even when wired, body editorial copy must NOT be auto-translated per invariant #10 — only these structural/chrome trust strings (which is what the design translates).

Transparency page divergence. The new design DELETES the `transparency` entry from the trust nav. Production still ships a full hardcoded transparency page containing INVENTED specific statistics (exact headcount, a precise revenue-mix percentage split, per-country government-request counts, third-party deliverability figures). The about-trust guide states /trust/transparency is Phase 2, auto-generated, with a Y1 placeholder of 'First report drops Q1 2027.' These invented numbers are an editorial-integrity risk (the product's core value) and contradict both the new design and the feature guide.

Dangling links to /trust/transparency. about/page.tsx TRUST_LINKS array (line ~85) and an inline body Link (line ~318) plus footer.tsx (line 30) all point to /trust/transparency. If the transparency slug is removed/placeholdered to follow the design, these references need to be updated or pointed at the Y1 placeholder, or they break/mislead.

Copy drift on multiple sections. Production retains pre-update copy the design changed: 'across Asia tech' (design broadens to 'across tech, in Asia and worldwide'); 'Pro membership revenue' (design drops 'Pro'); the 'See /trust/corrections.' inline pointer (design removed); the DTW-Studio sentence 'Writers, editors, and the EIC of DTW Studio do not work on the newsroom.' (design removed); 'Q4 transparency report' (design drops 'Q4'). Low visual impact but the copy is the point on a trust surface.
h1 weight mismatch: design softened the title from fontWeight 700 to 650; production still hardcodes 700.

AI Disclosure copy still describes the removed inline AI-assisted label. Both the new design AND production /trust/ai still state articles carry an 'AI-assisted' label at top/middle/bottom that cannot be turned off. Per invariant #5 the inline AI-assisted disclosure was removed by product decision 2026-06-05, and all-context.md flags this exact /trust/ai copy as a KNOWN GAP to reconcile. The new design does NOT fix it, so production should not be 'corrected' to match the design here — the policy copy itself needs a product decision before either is touched.

recommendedUpdates.change: Resolve the transparency page to match the new design + feature guide. Preferred: remove `transparency` from the TrustSlug union and PAGES map and replace it with the Y1 placeholder behavior — either drop it from the nav entirely (as the design does) OR render a single 'First report drops Q1 2027.' placeholder (per about-trust _GUIDE.md). Critically, delete the invented statistics regardless of which path is chosen. Confirm with product which of the two (remove vs placeholder) is wanted, since the design and the guide differ slightly.

Reconcile the dangling /trust/transparency links. If transparency is removed, drop the entry from about TRUST_LINKS, remove or repoint the inline about-body Link, and update the footer link (or point all three at the Y1 placeholder). Keep these in lockstep with the page change to avoid broken nav.

Add i18n to the trust pages to match the design intent (en/vi/id), using the existing useT() helper and t(en, vi, id) tuples for titles, kickers, section headings, the nav label, and the was/now correction labels. Because the page is an async Server Component that fetches corrections, split it: keep an async server wrapper that loads getCorrections() and passes plain data down, and move the presentational PAGES rendering into a 'use client' child that calls useT() (mirroring how about/page.tsx and awards/page.tsx are client components). Do NOT translate any article body copy — only these chrome/structural strings (invariant #10).

Apply the design's copy edits to the editorial and sponsored sections: broaden 'across Asia tech' to 'across tech, in Asia and worldwide'; change 'Pro membership revenue' to 'membership revenue'; drop the 'See /trust/corrections.' sentence; remove the 'Writers, editors, and the EIC of DTW Studio do not work on the newsroom.' sentence; change 'Q4 transparency report' to 'transparency report'. Pair this with the i18n update so the vi/id strings land at the same time.
Soften the h1 fontWeight from 700 to 650 to match the design (keep the production clamp() sizing — it's an improvement over the design's fixed 48px).

Do NOT auto-apply the AI-Disclosure copy. The design still describes the removed inline AI-assisted label, which conflicts with invariant #5. Surface to product: the /trust/ai page needs a policy decision (rewrite to reflect that the aiAssisted field is no longer surfaced inline) before changing either design or production. Treat this as a content/policy task, not a design-sync task.

invariantConflicts: Invariant #5 (AI-ASSISTED inline disclosure removed 2026-06-05): both the new design and production /trust/ai still describe an 'AI-assisted' label at top/middle/bottom that 'cannot be turned off'. all-context.md explicitly flags this /trust/ai copy as a KNOWN GAP. Do NOT reintroduce or reinforce inline AI badges; the page copy needs a product/policy decision, so neither the design nor production should be blindly synced here.

Invariant #9 (i18n year1 = en/id/vi subpath routing, never hardcode locale lists): the design's i18n is correct in spirit (en/vi/id via the shared t() helper), but note production uses a localStorage-backed language context, NOT the subpath routing (/en /id /vi) described by the invariant — there are no en/id/vi route segments in app/. This is a pre-existing whole-site i18n-strategy gap, not introduced by trust, but adding trust i18n via useT() continues down the localStorage path rather than subpath routing. Flag for the broader i18n decision; do not let the trust task silently entrench the non-subpath approach.

Invariant #10 (article body stays source language; only chrome translated): the design only translates structural/policy chrome strings on the trust surface, which is consistent. Any production implementation must keep this boundary — translate trust section headings/labels/kickers, never reader-authored article body copy.
openQuestions: Transparency page: should production REMOVE the slug entirely (new design) or render the Y1 placeholder 'First report drops Q1 2027.' (about-trust _GUIDE.md line 94)? The two authoritative sources differ. Either way the invented statistics must go.

i18n architecture: should the trust page adopt the existing localStorage-backed useT() (matching about/awards), or wait for the site-wide subpath-routing i18n (invariant #9) to land so trust isn't refactored twice?
AI Disclosure: has product finalised the post-2026-06-05 AI policy copy for /trust/ai? Until then, the AI-label description should not be synced from the design.

Should the corrections empty-state and 'Signed off by {editor}' enhancements (already in production, absent from the design) be preserved? Recommend yes — they are net improvements and Payload-backed.

compare.designChangeSummary: The about.jsx design was heavily rewritten (file is tracked; `git diff` shows a near-total rewrite). Four structural shifts: (1) BRAND RENAME — parent org changes from "Asia Press Corporation" / "Asia Press Corporation Pte. Ltd." to "Asia Press Centre Group (APCG)" across hero, body copy, EIC bio, masthead role, and footer biz-info; brand voice lowercases the title to "dailytechwire". (2) TWO-VARIANT COMPONENT — AboutPage now takes `variant="trust"|"newsroom"` (default trust). The default /about (trust) and a NEW newsroom variant render largely different sections. (3) HERO RESKIN — hero background moves from solid black var(--ink) to deep navy var(--banner) (#1B2A52) with text #E8EDF7; section heading top-borders move from 3px solid var(--ink) to 3px solid var(--brand-navy); all serif headings drop fontWeight 700->650. (4) CONTENT RESHAPE of the trust page — the 5 animated CountUp stat tiles (2023 / 8 / 210+ / 68% / 100%) are REPLACED by 3 qualitative "hero pillar" cards (heading+desc, no numbers); the "Ownership & funding" section (OWNERSHIP table + REVENUE bar/breakdown) is REMOVED from the trust page; the Mission section loses its intro paragraph and tightens padding (56px->40px 44px); EIC profile + Masthead + Bureaus now live only in the newsroom variant (EIC loses the CoverArt portrait block; masthead loses the "·Ny on the beat" suffix); the "Transparency Report" entry is removed from both the trust quick-links list and the "Who we are" copy; footer biz-info shrinks 4->3 columns with new content (Asia Press Centre Group / Bugis Cube / Singapore 188735; media@/editor@/partnership@dailytechwire.com; asiapresscentre.com) and drops UEN/GST/MICA/investor-relations. Accent and category colors in BEATS/REVENUE are re-toned (accent softened to #D4623C in design tokens; tech color #E04E1F->#B0512E). Newsroom-only data arrays BEATS and BUREAUS are now actually used (previously dead).
compare.currentProductionSummary: Production apps/web/src/app/(reader)/about/page.tsx is a single-variant client component matching the PREVIOUS design (pre-rewrite). It renders, in order: a black (var(--ink)) hero with 5 animated CountUp stat tiles, "Who we are" (Asia Press Corporation, founded 2023, eight publications, links to /trust/transparency), Mission & values (with intro paragraph + 4 value cards), Editor-in-Chief profile (CoverArt portrait placeholder + bio referencing Group Editor of all eight publications + credentials grid), Masthead (8 people with "city · Ny on the beat"), Ownership & funding (OWNERSHIP table + REVENUE bar chart, FY2025), Trust quick-links (5 entries incl. Transparency Report), an encrypted Tip line, and a 4-column footer biz-info (Asia Press Corporation Pte. Ltd., 61 Robinson Road, UEN/GST/MICA, press@/ir@asiapress.com). It uses only existing CSS vars (--ink, --paper, --accent); --banner and --brand-navy are NOT defined in apps/web/src/app/globals.css. There is no newsroom variant and no /newsroom route. Production already improved responsiveness over the design (clamp() font sizes, auto-fit minmax grids) — keep these.

gaps.description: Brand rename not applied: production still says 'Asia Press Corporation' / 'Asia Press Corporation Pte. Ltd.' in hero kicker, hero intro (3 langs), Who-we-are body, EIC bio + role line, masthead role, and footer registered-office. Design renames all to 'Asia Press Centre Group (APCG)'. This contradicts CLAUDE.md, process/context/all-context.md, and process/features/about-trust/_GUIDE.md, which all still authoritatively say 'Asia Press Corporation' — a source-of-truth conflict that must be resolved before mechanically applying copy.

Hero background reskin missing: design uses deep-navy var(--banner) (#1B2A52) + text #E8EDF7; production uses solid black var(--ink). The --banner and --brand-navy CSS variables do not exist in apps/web/src/app/globals.css (light or dark), so the design cannot be applied without first adding these tokens.
Section heading top-borders use 3px solid var(--ink) in production; design switches to var(--brand-navy). Same missing-token blocker.

Trust-page content reshape not reflected: design replaces the 5 CountUp stat tiles with 3 qualitative hero-pillar cards, removes the entire Ownership & funding (OWNERSHIP/REVENUE) section from /about, removes the Mission intro paragraph, and removes the Transparency Report from the quick-links list and Who-we-are copy. Production still renders all of these. Note transparency tension: removing the public revenue/ownership breakdown reduces disclosure — confirm intended direction.

Newsroom variant has no home: design adds a variant='newsroom' mode (Inside-the-newsroom hero, 'What we cover' BEATS grid, EIC profile without CoverArt, full masthead, Bureaus & desks). Production exposes no /newsroom route and the BEATS/BUREAUS arrays do not exist in production. Decide whether /newsroom is a wanted new page before building it.

Footer biz-info drift: production has 4 columns (registered office at 61 Robinson Road, UEN/GST/MICA particulars, press@asiapress.com, ir@asiapress.com / investor relations). Design has 3 columns with new address (Bugis Cube, Singapore 188735), media@/editor@/partnership@dailytechwire.com, asiapresscentre.com, and drops the company-particulars + investor-relations blocks.

EIC bio factual drift: design softens '20+ years / two-plus decades / Group Editor of all eight publications' to 'a long career / veteran editor / sets the title's direction', drops the masthead 'Ny on the beat' suffix and the 'Career: Two-plus decades' credential. Keeping fabricated tenure claims out aligns with the all-context warning against invented career history, so the softer copy is likely safer.
Tip-line / contact emails changed: production tips@/corrections@dailytechwire.asia -> design media@/editor@dailytechwire.com. Cosmetic but should be reconciled with whatever real addresses exist.

recommendedUpdates.change: Resolve the brand-name source-of-truth conflict FIRST (do not silently apply). Decide whether 'Asia Press Centre Group (APCG)' supersedes 'Asia Press Corporation'. If yes, update the canonical docs (all-context.md parent-org section, CLAUDE.md project description, about-trust/_GUIDE.md) in the same change, then propagate the rename through the about page copy. If no, keep production copy and treat the design rename as not-yet-approved.

Add --banner (#1B2A52 light / #16223C dark) and --brand-navy (#1B2A52 light / #E2E8F0 dark) CSS variables to globals.css mirroring the design index.html tokens, then switch the about hero background + section top-borders from var(--ink) to var(--banner)/var(--brand-navy) with #E8EDF7 text. Audit other surfaces (footer, other trust pages) that may also expect these new banner tokens.

Reshape the trust /about page to the new design: replace the 5 CountUp stat tiles with 3 qualitative hero-pillar cards; remove the Ownership & funding (OWNERSHIP/REVENUE) section; drop the Mission intro paragraph and tighten padding; remove the Transparency Report quick-link and the transparency reference in Who-we-are copy. (2) Font weights were softened from 700 to 650 on the avatar initial, the user name H1, and every section H2 heading. (3) Most significantly, the entire Pro upgrade button was REMOVED from the account header — the previous design rendered `<Button variant={user.pro?"primary":"accent"}>{user.pro ? "DTW Pro · active" : "Upgrade to Pro · $12/mo"}</Button>` and that whole element is now deleted, leaving the header with just the avatar + name/email/role block. The new account header carries no Pro CTA or Pro-status pill at all. Tab structure (saved/history/following/newsletters/settings), the 220px sidebar nav layout, profile header, and per-tab content are otherwise unchanged.

Production never had the design's prior user.pro conditional (the User type in lib/shell.tsx has no `pro` boolean, only role which can equal "Pro"), so it always shows "Upgrade to Pro". This is exactly the element the new design deleted. Production heading/avatar/name font weights are still 700 (design softened them to 650).
