compare.designChangeSummary: The git diff on design/project/src/search.jsx is purely an i18n localization pass — no structural or visual change. The previous version had hardcoded English strings; the new version wires every chrome string through the t(en, vi, id) helper and useLang(): the "Search" kicker, the input placeholder, the "matches" count label, the three facet block titles (Pillar/Date/Type), every facet option label, the Typesense help-box body copy, the "Trending searches" heading, the no-results headline + body, the pillar facet labels (now localizedPillarLabel(p, lang) instead of pillarOf(p).label), and the result-card "By … min" byline. The page layout/structure is unchanged from the prior design: a 220px facet sidebar (sticky, top:160) + 1fr results grid; a large serif search input with a "{n} matches · 134ms" timing readout; three facet groups (Pillar with colored dot + per-pillar counts, Date radio list, Type radio list); a "Powered by Typesense / Typo-tolerant. EN · ID · VI" info box; a trending-searches chip cloud shown when the query is empty; a dashed-border no-results card whose copy says empty queries are logged so editors know what's missing; and result rows of CoverArt (160px) + pillar tag + section + date + serif title + dek + byline.
compare.currentProductionSummary: Production already implements the search page (apps/web/src/app/(reader)/search/page.tsx) and the ⌘K overlay (apps/web/src/components/search-overlay.tsx), and it is functionally AHEAD of the prototype. It is fully internationalized via the same t(en,vi,id) helper and localizedPillarLabel(p, lang) — so the design's i18n change (the only delta in this diff) is already satisfied. The page uses the responsive .r-sidebar grid class (220px 1fr, collapsing to 1fr under 768px via globals.css), matching the design's two-column layout while adding mobile stacking the prototype lacks. Search is real, not mock: a debounced (220ms) server action runSearch -> searchArticles() queries Payload for published articles where title OR dek LIKE the query, sorted by -publishedAt, depth 1. Result cards render CoverArt (with real heroImageUrl), localized PillarTag, section, localized date (fmtDateL), serif title, dek, and a localized "By {author} · {readMin} min · <TimeAgo>" line. Pillar facet works fully (client-side narrowing of the returned set with live per-pillar counts). Empty-query state shows a localized "Try searching" chip cloud (SUGGESTED keywords). No-results shows a localized dashed-border card. Loading shows a localized "Searching…" status. Notable PRODUCTION DECISIONS that diverge from the prototype: (1) only the Pillar facet exists — the Date and Type facets are not rendered; (2) the info box copy is honest about current capability ("Searches published article titles and standfirsts. Typo-tolerant search and more filters are coming.") rather than claiming Typesense; (3) the "{n} matches · 134ms" fake timing readout is intentionally dropped (production shows just "{n} matches" / "Searching…"); (4) the no-results copy does NOT claim empty queries are logged; (5) trending/suggested keywords are a static curated list, not "trending". The overlay mirrors the page: Cmd/Ctrl+K open (via useShell), Esc to close, debounced runSearch (200ms, top 8 hits), Enter routes to /search?q=…, localized strings throughout.

gaps.description: The ONLY change in this design diff (i18n localization of all chrome strings) is already fully implemented in production. There is no new gap introduced by this design update. The search page and overlay already use t(en,vi,id) and localizedPillarLabel for every chrome string the design localized.

Pre-existing (NOT introduced by this diff): the design renders Date and Type facet blocks (Any time/24h/7d/30d/12m; Anything/Articles/Dashboards/Authors/Awards). Production renders only the Pillar facet. This is a deliberate deferral — production's own info-box copy says 'more filters are coming.' Aligns with the feature spec (faceted search with pillar/date/type), so it is a real but already-acknowledged future-work gap, not a regression.

Pre-existing: design's no-results copy promises 'We log empty queries so editors know what we're missing.' Production neither logs zero-result queries nor makes that claim. PostHog is not wired anywhere in the app (no posthog import in apps/web/src). The integrations/search feature spec calls for PostHog zero-result query analytics. Production's honest copy avoids the broken promise, so this is a deferred analytics feature, not a UI mismatch caused by this diff.

Pre-existing: design's info box says 'Powered by Typesense / Typo-tolerant.' Production uses a Postgres LIKE query via Payload (not typo-tolerant, no Meilisearch/Typesense index yet) and its copy correctly says typo-tolerant search 'is coming.' Search engine choice (Meilisearch vs Typesense) is an explicitly open decision in all-context.md. No action needed for this diff.

Pre-existing cosmetic: design shows a '· 134ms' timing readout next to the match count. Production intentionally omits it (a fake static number would be misleading; a real timing would require instrumenting the server action). Not worth implementing.

Pre-existing: design scopes search across 'articles, dashboards, awards, and people'; the empty placeholder reads 'Search stories, dashboards, authors, awards…'. Production searches only published articles and uses the honest placeholder 'Search published stories…'. Multi-entity indexing is future work per the feature spec.

recommendedUpdates.change: No update required to satisfy this design diff. Production already localizes every string the design localized. Confirm the localizedPillarLabel/t wiring renders correctly under id and vi locales as a quick visual check, but no code change is needed.

OPTIONAL future work (not this diff): add Date and Type facet blocks to the search page to match the design's three-facet layout. Date would require passing a date-range param to runSearch/searchArticles and adding a publishedAt range to the Payload where clause; Type requires multi-collection search (articles/dashboards/authors/awards) which does not exist yet. Keep the honest info-box copy until these land. Reuse the existing FacetBlock component.

OPTIONAL future work (not this diff): wire PostHog (self-hosted, per invariant 12) zero-result query logging in runSearch / the search page so editors can see what readers fail to find, then the design's 'we log empty queries' copy becomes true. PostHog is not yet integrated anywhere in apps/web, so this is net-new infra, not a search-only change.

invariantConflicts: Invariant 9 (i18n year1 = en/id/vi, never hardcode locale lists): NO conflict — fully satisfied. The design's i18n change and production both use the t(en,vi,id) helper and localizedPillarLabel(p,lang) rather than hardcoded locale arrays.

Invariant 12 (PostHog self-hosted, reader data residency): NO conflict introduced. Flagging only that the design's 'we log empty queries' copy implies analytics that production has correctly NOT yet implemented; if implemented later it must use the self-hosted PostHog instance.

Note on the 'Powered by Typesense' design copy vs all-context.md Open Decision (Meilisearch is the current default, Typesense the alternate): not an invariant violation, but the design hardcodes 'Typesense' while the open decision still favors Meilisearch. Production sidesteps this by not naming an engine. No action needed.
openQuestions: Search engine for Y1 remains an open decision (Meilisearch default vs Typesense) per all-context.md — the design's 'Powered by Typesense' copy presumes Typesense. Should production copy/branding align to whichever engine is chosen before the help-box text is finalized?

Are the Date and Type facets and multi-entity (dashboards/authors/awards) search in scope for the current phase, or do they stay deferred behind the 'more filters are coming' copy? The design clearly wants all three facets.
Should zero-result query logging (PostHog, self-hosted) be prioritized so the design's editor-facing 'we log empty queries' promise can be honestly surfaced in the no-results copy?

compare.designChangeSummary: The git diff on design/project/src/auth.jsx is almost entirely whitespace/formatting normalization (rgba spacing, shorthand array/effect-dep formatting). Only THREE substantive content changes landed: (1) the signup headline changed from "Read what matters in Asia tech." to "Read what matters in tech, across Asia and the world" and the login headline lost its trailing period ("Welcome back." -> "Welcome back"); (2) the signup footer prompt copy changed "New to DailyTechWire?" -> "New to dailytechwire?" (lowercase wordmark, matching the lowercase wordmark used elsewhere in the design); (3) a search-overlay "no matches" hint changed the pillar example from "AI, Asia, Dev" to "AI, Latest, Dev" (Asia pillar renamed to Latest). The SearchOverlay component in this same file is unrelated to the auth surface. The overall AuthModal design intent is unchanged: a 440px centered modal with a Wordmark at top-left, an X close button, a serif headline that flips between login/signup modes, a magic-link email form, an "or continue with" divider, three side-by-side OAuth buttons (Google/Apple/GitHub), a sent-confirmation state ("Check your inbox … expires in 15 minutes"), a login<->signup toggle link, and fine print noting Terms/Privacy plus "Admins and editors require 2FA". Magic-link / no-passwords positioning is core to the design.
compare.currentProductionSummary: apps/web/src/components/auth-modal.tsx is a Phase-1 stub explicitly documented as faking login until Better-Auth lands in Phase 2. It renders a ~420px centered modal with an absolute-positioned X (Icon name="close"), a "kicker" eyebrow ("Welcome to DTW") plus a serif "Sign in" headline (i18n via useT for en/vi/id), a magic-link email form (Button variant="accent" "Send magic link"), an "or continue with" divider, and three FULL-WIDTH stacked OAuth buttons labelled "Continue with Google/Apple/GitHub". It has NO login/signup mode switch (no mode prop, no toggle link, no signup headline/subcopy), NO sent/"check your inbox" confirmation state (it instantly calls demoLogin and closes), NO 15-minute-expiry messaging, NO Wordmark in the header, and NO "Admins and editors require 2FA" / Terms-Privacy fine print (instead it shows a demo-only disclaimer). State is driven by useShell() (authOpen/openAuth/closeAuth/setUser) — openAuth takes no mode argument, so login-vs-signup intent cannot be passed from header or paywall. The User type in lib/shell.tsx has no `pro` boolean (design sets pro:false on login). ESC-to-close is NOT implemented in the modal (the design's AuthModal listens for Escape); only the search overlay path has a global ⌘K listener in shell. Overlay uses backdrop color-mix(var(--ink) 50%) with no blur (design uses rgba(17,17,17,.55) + backdropFilter blur(2px)). The modal is mounted once in app/(reader)/layout.tsx and triggered from header.tsx (openAuth) and from the paywall in article-content.tsx (Paywall onLogin={openAuth}).

gaps.description: No login/signup mode toggle. Design wants a two-mode modal (distinct headline + subcopy for each, plus a footer link to switch). Production has a single hardcoded 'Sign in' view with no signup affordance and no way to pass mode from the trigger (openAuth takes no argument; shell has no authMode state).

No 'Check your inbox' sent/confirmation state. Design shows a success panel ('We sent a sign-in link to <email>. It expires in 15 minutes.') after submit. We don't use passwords.' / signup subcopy 'Create a free account to save articles, follow pillars, and sync across devices.', and the toggle prompt copy. Production uses generic 'Welcome to DTW' / 'Sign in' / demo disclaimer instead.

Missing fine print 'By continuing you agree to our Terms and Privacy.' and 'Admins and editors require 2FA.' The 2FA note ties to the RBAC requirement that Admin/Editor need 2FA (auth context). Production shows only a demo disclaimer.

No Wordmark at top-left of the modal. Design renders <Wordmark size={22}> as the modal's brand mark; production shows a text 'kicker' eyebrow only. (Note: the Wordmark in this design is the navy 'DTW' badge + lowercase wordmark — see invariant #11 note below.)

OAuth button layout differs: design uses a 3-column grid of compact buttons labelled just 'Google'/'Apple'/'GitHub'; production stacks three full-width 'Continue with X' buttons. Visual/structural divergence, not functional.
ESC-to-close not implemented on the production AuthModal (design adds a keydown Escape listener). Backdrop click-to-close works in both. Minor a11y/UX gap.
Overlay visual: design uses a darker scrim rgba(17,17,17,.55) with backdropFilter blur(2px); production uses color-mix(var(--ink) 50%) and no blur. Cosmetic.

recommendedUpdates.change: Add login/signup mode support: introduce authMode state ('login'|'signup') and an openAuth(mode?) signature in the shell, render a footer toggle link, and switch headline/subcopy per mode. Update header.tsx and article-content.tsx paywall trigger to pass the intended mode (e.g. paywall -> 'signup').

Update modal copy to match the new design strings (login/signup headlines and subcopy, toggle prompt) and add the fine print 'By continuing you agree to our Terms and Privacy.' + 'Admins and editors require 2FA.' Route all strings through useT for en/vi/id per invariant #9/#10 (chrome is translatable).

Add ESC-to-close keydown listener to the AuthModal (mirror the search overlay pattern) and align the backdrop scrim with the design (darker scrim + optional backdrop blur), keeping CSS-variable theming so dark mode adapts.
Optional: switch OAuth buttons to the 3-column compact grid layout to match the design, or consciously keep the stacked full-width variant. Low priority cosmetic alignment.

invariantConflicts: Invariant #11 (no logo badge in header — only the wordmark): The design's <Wordmark> used in the auth modal renders a navy rounded-rect 'DTW' BADGE alongside the 'dailytechwire' wordmark (design/project/src/header.jsx lines 21-24). Should the recommended UX updates (mode toggle, sent-state, copy/fine-print) be applied NOW to the stub, or deferred to the Phase-2 Better-Auth integration so the stub isn't reworked twice?

The design's User model carries a `pro` boolean (pro:false on login); production's User type omits it. Is Pro tier in scope for the auth surface yet, or intentionally deferred?
Should magic-link/OAuth copy be fully translated via useT (en/vi/id) to satisfy i18n invariants, or kept English-only while the auth flow is a demo stub?

compare.designChangeSummary: The new design/project/src/ui.jsx has exactly TWO committed deltas vs the prior version (confirmed via git diff): (1) DisclosureBox now wraps ALL of its copy in the t(en, vi, id) i18n helper — for both the sponsored variant (title "Paid Partner" -> "Đối tác trả phí" / "Mitra Berbayar"; full sponsored-firewall body) AND the AI variant (title "AI-assisted reporting" + body). Previously these were hardcoded English. (2) Button primary variant background/borderColor changed from var(--ink) to var(--brand-navy). All other primitives (PillarTag, ByLine, Spark, ArrowUpDown, Placeholder, Skeleton) are byte-for-byte unchanged in this design revision. Net design intent: disclosure boxes are chrome and must localize with the reader's language; the primary button should read in the brand navy rather than near-black ink.
compare.currentProductionSummary: Shared primitives live in packages/ui/src/ (not the hint paths, which are wrappers). They are typed, presentational React components that visually mirror the design 1:1. DisclosureBox (packages/ui/src/disclosure-box.tsx) is English-only: it has DEFAULT_SPONSORED_BODY / DEFAULT_AI_BODY constants and resolves title to "Paid Partner · {sponsor}" or "AI-assisted reporting" unless title/body props are passed. It DOES accept optional title/body overrides, but the only consumer — apps/web/src/components/article/article-body.tsx — renders it with no localized props, so disclosure copy is always English even when chrome language is vi/id. Button (packages/ui/src/button.tsx) primary variant is still { background: var(--ink), borderColor: var(--ink) }. The --brand-navy token is NOT defined in apps/web/src/app/globals.css (it exists only in the design's index.html: light #1B2A52, dark #E2E8F0). Byline is already i18n-wired via the BylineWired wrapper (apps/web/src/components/byline-wired.tsx) which feeds useT() labels. The production useT() signature in apps/web/src/lib/i18n.tsx is (en, vi?, id?) — an exact match for the design's t(). Critically, article-body.tsx STILL renders <DisclosureBox kind="ai" ...> at top/middle/bottom gated on article.aiAssisted, which invariant #5 says was removed on 2026-06-05.

gaps.description: DisclosureBox copy is not localized in production. The new design localizes the sponsored title + body into vi/id, but production renders English-only strings for all readers. Since disclosure boxes are chrome (not article body), invariant #10 requires them to be translated. The primitive already supports title/body overrides, so the gap is purely at the consumer call site in article-body.tsx, which passes no localized strings.

Button primary variant color mismatch. Design changed primary to var(--brand-navy); production still uses var(--ink). This is a small visual drift (near-black vs navy #1B2A52). It is currently low-impact but will diverge further if the brand navy becomes the standard CTA color.

Blocking dependency for the Button change: --brand-navy is undefined in apps/web/src/app/globals.css. Porting the design's primary={ background: var(--brand-navy) } as-is would resolve to an empty/invalid background and break the primary button. The token (light #1B2A52, dark #E2E8F0) must be added to globals.css :root and html[data-theme=dark] AND mapped into the @theme inline block first.

The design's DisclosureBox still contains the AI variant (isAI, kind='ai', 'AI' icon) and the design's ONLY change to it was to translate it — which would re-entrench an inline disclosure that product policy removed on 2026-06-05. Production's article-body.tsx already (still) renders kind='ai' boxes at top/middle/bottom. The design is stale on this point; the AI localization must NOT be ported, and the existing AI rendering should be removed from production. See invariantConflicts.

recommendedUpdates.change: Localize the sponsored DisclosureBox at its call site. In article-body.tsx, replace the bare <DisclosureBox kind="sponsored" .../> calls with localized title/body via useT(): title = t("Paid Partner", "Đối tác trả phí", "Mitra Berbayar") + (sponsor ? " · "+sponsor : ""), body = the t(en, vi, id) sponsored-firewall strings copied verbatim from design ui.jsx. Make the component a client wrapper (a 'DisclosureBoxWired' like BylineWired) or thread useT()-derived strings down, since article-body.tsx is already a 'use client' component. Do NOT touch the disclosure-box.tsx primitive — it already accepts title/body overrides.

Add the --brand-navy token to production tokens, then switch Button primary to use it. Step 1: in apps/web/src/app/globals.css add --brand-navy:#1B2A52 to :root and --brand-navy:#E2E8F0 to html[data-theme="dark"], and add --color-brand-navy: var(--brand-navy) to the @theme inline block. Step 2: in packages/ui/src/button.tsx change the primary variant to { background: var(--brand-navy), color: var(--paper), borderColor: var(--brand-navy) }. Step 1 MUST land before/with Step 2 or the button background breaks.

Reconcile invariant #5 against the stale design: remove inline AI-assisted disclosure rendering from production rather than porting the design's AI localization. In article-body.tsx delete the three {article.aiAssisted && <DisclosureBox kind="ai" .../>} branches (top/middle/bottom) and simplify the `disclosed` gate to article.sponsored only. Optionally deprecate the kind="ai" branch in disclosure-box.tsx. The aiAssisted field stays on the Article model (Engine still sets it) — only the inline surface is removed. Confirm with product before deleting, since this is a policy-driven divergence from the design bundle.

invariantConflicts: Invariant #5: The new design's DisclosureBox still ships the AI-assisted variant (kind='ai', 'AI-assisted reporting') and this design revision merely localizes it — but invariant #5 states the AI-assisted inline disclosure (header AI badge + the three kind='ai' DisclosureBoxes) was REMOVED by product decision 2026-06-05 and must NOT be reintroduced. Production article-body.tsx STILL renders <DisclosureBox kind='ai' position='top|middle|bottom' /> gated on article.aiAssisted — this is an active violation that should be removed, and the design's AI-localization change must NOT be ported.

Invariant #10: Disclosure boxes are article chrome, not body, so they must be translated. Production currently renders sponsored disclosure copy in English only regardless of reader language (en/id/vi), which conflicts with the 'only chrome is translated, and it IS translated' intent. The design change (localizing the sponsored box) is the correct direction and aligned with #10 — implementing it resolves the conflict. (Note: the localized strings affect only the disclosure chrome, not article body, so #10's 'body stays source language' rule is preserved.)
openQuestions: Is the AI-assisted inline disclosure removal (invariant #5) intended to be enforced in this iteration, given that the design bundle still ships the AI variant and the production article-body.tsx still renders it? Need product confirmation before deleting the kind='ai' branches, since the design appears stale rather than authoritative on this point.

Should --brand-navy become the canonical CTA/primary color across the app (header CTAs, auth modal, footer newsletter button), or is the Button primary change scoped only to the shared primitive? This affects whether other components currently using var(--ink) buttons should also shift.

The sponsored disclosure border uses a hardcoded #E0B900 (gold) in both design and production rather than a token. Out of scope for this change, but flag whether it should be tokenized for dark-mode adaptation consistency with the var(--...) theming discipline.

compare.designChangeSummary: The git diff on design/project/src/icons.jsx shows exactly two changes (file is tracked; not new). (1) A NEW icon case was added: `clock` — a clock face = `<circle cx=12 cy=12 r=9>` plus a clock-hand `<polyline points="12 7 12 12 15 14">`, using the same stroke conventions as the rest of the set. (2) The `PILLAR_ICONS` map was retargeted: the entry `asia: "asia"` was removed and `latest: "clock"` was added. Cross-checking design/project/src/data.jsx confirms this is part of a broader taxonomy rename: the first pillar changed from `{ id:"asia", icon "asia" }` to `{ id:"latest", label:"Latest", color:"var(--asia)", slug:"/latest" }` — it keeps the same coral/--asia color but is now the "Latest" pillar with the clock icon. The `asia` icon GLYPH itself is unchanged and still present in the icon set; only its assignment in PILLAR_ICONS was dropped. No icons were deleted, restyled, or had their geometry changed.
compare.currentProductionSummary: Production ports the icon set to apps/web/src/components/icons.tsx as a typed React component (IconName union + Icon({name,size,color,stroke})). All 31 design glyphs match production 1:1 in geometry, viewBox (0 0 24 24), stroke defaults (1.75, round caps/joins), and the play/pause/rss/info/wire fill exceptions — production is faithful. However production is MISSING the new `clock` case: there is no `clock` branch in the switch and no `"clock"` member in the IconName union (the design diff added it but production was not updated). PILLAR_ICONS was structurally relocated out of icons.tsx into apps/web/src/lib/data.ts (a reasonable adaptation), where it still reads `Record<PillarId,string>` with `asia: "asia"` and PillarId still includes "asia" (no "latest"). PILLARS in lib/data.ts still defines `{ id:"asia", label:"Asia", color:"var(--asia)", slug:"/asia" }`. The `--asia: #E04E1F` CSS var and `--color-asia` exist in globals.css. The Payload Pillars collection (payload/collections/Pillars.ts) stores `icon` as a free-text field whose admin description lists allowed names "spark | trend-up | asia | code | product | policy" and the slug description references "ai|startups|asia|dev|products|policy" — both still reference asia, neither mentions latest/clock. PILLAR_ICONS[p.id] is consumed in header.tsx (lines 543, 758), home/pillar-showcase.tsx (line 66); pillar pages pass pillarIcon from pillarDoc.icon (Payload) to pillar-content.tsx.

gaps.description: The new `clock` icon glyph is absent from production icons.tsx. The switch has no `clock` case and the IconName union lacks `"clock"`. Any CMS pillar (or PILLAR_ICONS entry) set to icon `clock` would fall through to the default placeholder circle. This is the only change that lives strictly inside the icons surface and must be ported.

PILLAR_ICONS in lib/data.ts still maps `asia: "asia"` and has no `latest` entry, while the design retargets the first pillar to `latest: "clock"`. This is the icons-side reflection of a pillar taxonomy rename (asia -> Latest). Until the pillar taxonomy is decided, the icon mapping cannot be finalized.

Broader taxonomy divergence (out of pure icons scope but the root cause): design data.jsx renamed pillar `asia` -> `latest` (label "Latest", slug /latest, retains var(--asia) color). Production still ships pillar `asia` in lib/data.ts PILLARS, PillarId type, and the Payload Pillars collection admin/slug descriptions. Because Pillars are CMS entities (invariant #8), the live rename is a CMS data edit, but the supporting code references (PillarId type, fixture PILLARS, doc strings) must be reconciled. The `asia` icon glyph should be KEPT (still a valid selectable icon), only its PILLAR_ICONS assignment changes.

recommendedUpdates.change: Add the `clock` icon to the production icon set: insert a `case "clock": return (<svg {...common}><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="9" /><polyline points="12 7 12 12 15 14" /></svg>);` branch (place it after `info`, mirroring the design diff position) and add `| "clock"` to the IconName union. This is a pure-additive port of the diff's new glyph; no existing icon changes.

Reconcile the pillar->icon mapping to match design intent (asia pillar -> latest). If/when the pillar is renamed: in lib/data.ts change the PILLARS entry to `{ id:"latest", label:"Latest", color:"var(--asia)", slug:"/latest" }`, update the PillarId union (`asia` -> `latest`), and change PILLAR_ICONS `asia: "asia"` to `latest: "clock"`. Keep the `--asia` CSS var (color is reused) and keep the `asia` icon glyph in icons.tsx (still selectable). Note: PILLARS/PILLAR_ICONS here are fixture data slated for CMS replacement, so confirm whether production should track this rename in fixtures now or defer to the CMS edit.

Update the Payload Pillars collection admin documentation strings to reflect the renamed taxonomy and the new selectable icon: the slug description (ai|startups|asia|...) and the icon description (spark | trend-up | asia | ... ) should mention `latest`/`clock` per the design's intent. The icon field is free-text so no schema migration is required, but the live `asia` Pillar document's slug/title/icon would need an editorial CMS edit (icon -> clock, slug -> latest) to take effect — that is a CMS data change, not code (invariant #8).

invariantConflicts: Invariant #8 (Pillar/Sub-section/Tag are CMS entities; routes regenerate from CMS): the design's asia->latest pillar rename and icon swap is fundamentally a CMS data edit on the live Pillar document (slug, title, icon=clock), NOT a code deploy. Code-side changes (icons.tsx clock glyph, lib/data.ts fixtures, Pillars.ts doc strings) only enable/support it. Flagging so the rename is applied through Payload rather than hardcoded as a deploy-time route change. No conflict if handled as a CMS edit; would conflict if someone hardcodes pillar routing instead of letting CMS regenerate routes/sitemap/RSS.

Invariant #7/#11 sanity check (no conflict): the `clock` glyph and the retained `asia`/coral `--asia: #E04E1F` color do not touch fixed brand colors or reintroduce a header logo badge. The icons.jsx diff introduces no logo/badge and no AI-assisted iconography, so invariants #5 and #11 are not implicated.
openQuestions: Should the asia->latest pillar rename be reflected in the production fixture data (lib/data.ts PILLARS/PillarId) now, or deferred entirely to a CMS edit on the live Pillar document since these fixtures are flagged for Payload replacement?
Is the existing `asia` icon glyph being retired from the selectable set, or kept available for editors? (Design keeps the glyph and only drops its PILLAR_ICONS assignment — recommend keeping it.)

Does the live Payload `asia` Pillar document need its slug changed to `latest` (which changes the public route /asia -> /latest and requires redirect handling), or only its label and icon? This affects routing, sitemap, and any existing inbound links.

compare.designChangeSummary:
The new art.jsx is a tracked-file modification (git diff is non-empty), not a brand-new file. Three substantive changes vs the previous design version, plus cosmetic-only churn:

SUBSTANTIVE:
1. Cover-art label badge REMOVED. The previous design rendered an absolute bottom-left mono uppercase chip when a `label` prop was passed (white .85 bg, 1px border, radius 3). The new design deletes that block entirely and replaces it with the comment `{/* image label intentionally hidden */}`. Design intent: cover art should be clean with no overlaid text badge.
2. Avatar monogram palette DESATURATED. Old bright palette ["#E04E1F","#0EA5E9","#7C3AED","#16A34A","#D97706","#475569"] -> new muted/editorial palette ["#B0512E","#3E6E80","#3A4E8C","#46735C","#8F7238","#475569"]. Avatar font-weight also softened 700 -> 650.
3. CityChip CITY_COLOR dots DESATURATED to match the new muted avatar palette (e.g. Singapore #E04E1F->#B0512E, Seoul #7C3AED->#3A4E8C, Jakarta #0EA5E9->#3E6E80, Hanoi/HCMC #16A34A->#46735C, Taipei/Manila #D97706->#8F7238, Bangkok #7C3AED->#3A4E8C; Tokyo/Hong Kong #475569 and Shenzhen #7C2D12 unchanged).

COSMETIC ONLY (no behavior): en-dash -> comma in 3 header comments; whitespace `w = 800,hh` -> `w = 800, hh`; one extra space inside the v===4 path-string template. ART_PALETTES (the cover-art SVG color sets) are UNCHANGED.
compare.currentProductionSummary: apps/web/src/components/cover-art.tsx is a faithful, well-typed RSC port of the PREVIOUS design version, exporting CoverArt, Avatar, BrandMark, CityChip. It already adds production-grade hardening the design lacks: a `src` real-image branch (renders when a hero photo exists, generative SVG is the fallback) and SSR/hydration float-rounding (round2) in variants 3 and 4 to avoid Node-vs-browser libm mismatch. It still renders the OLD label badge via a `CoverLabel` helper in BOTH the image branch and the generative branch. Avatar still uses the OLD bright palette and font-weight 700. CityChip still uses the OLD bright CITY_COLOR map. The only call site passing a label is components/home/home-hero.tsx (`label="DTW HERO"`); 11 other CoverArt call sites pass no label and are visually unaffected by the label change.

gaps.description: Label badge still rendered in production. cover-art.tsx defines CoverLabel and calls it in both the src/image branch (line 108) and generative branch (line 308); the new design removed this overlay entirely. Currently visible on the homepage hero as a 'DTW HERO' chip (home-hero.tsx passes label="DTW HERO").

Avatar monogram palette not desaturated. Production palette at cover-art.tsx line 325 is the old bright set; new design wants the muted editorial set. Affects author avatars wherever rendered (byline, related rows, account).
Avatar font-weight is 700 in production (line 341); new design softened to 650.

CityChip CITY_COLOR dots not desaturated. Production map at cover-art.tsx lines 404-417 is the old bright set; new design wants the muted set. Affects city markers (e.g. Asia Funding / spotlight surfaces).

recommendedUpdates.change: Remove the cover-art label overlay to match the new design ('image label intentionally hidden'). Delete the CoverLabel helper and its two call sites in cover-art.tsx. DECISION NEEDED: the production `src` (real-image) branch also uses the label as the — when dropping the visible badge, repurpose `label` as alt text only (keep alt for a11y/WCAG AA, just stop rendering the visible chip) rather than removing the prop. Also drop the now-unused label="DTW HERO" from home-hero.tsx (or keep it solely for alt). Lowest-risk path: keep the `label` prop + alt usage, delete only the visible CoverLabel render.
Update the Avatar monogram palette array to the new muted editorial set ["#B0512E","#3E6E80","#3A4E8C","#46735C","#8F7238","#475569"] and change Avatar fontWeight 700 -> 650.

Update the CITY_COLOR map to the new muted dot colors (Singapore #B0512E, Seoul #3A4E8C, Jakarta #3E6E80, Hanoi/Ho Chi Minh City #46735C, Taipei #8F7238, Bengaluru #3E6E80, Manila #8F7238, Bangkok #3A4E8C; Tokyo/Hong Kong #475569 and Shenzhen #7C2D12 unchanged).

invariantConflicts: Invariant #7 (brand colors fixed): NOT violated, but watch the WCAG-AA contrast target (>=4.5:1) when desaturating — the new muted avatar/city colors are darker so contrast against white text/--ink should be fine, but verify CityChip dot/Avatar text contrast after the swap. The coral accent #E04E1F is removed from the Avatar/City palettes by design; #E04E1F remains correctly present in ART_PALETTES (asia pillar) and is unaffected.

Invariant #11 (no logo badge in header): The design bundle ships dtw-logo-primary.svg, but art.jsx does NOT introduce a header logo — it only removes a cover-art text badge. No conflict from this surface.
openQuestions: Label removal: should the visible cover badge be dropped entirely (design intent) while preserving `label` as the for the real-image branch (a11y/WCAG)? Recommend keeping alt text and removing only the visible chip; confirm before deleting the `label` prop.
Should home-hero.tsx keep label="DTW HERO" purely as alt text, or drop it? If kept, no other call sites are affected since they pass no label.

Desaturated palettes apply in both light and dark mode (Avatar/CityChip use fixed hex, not CSS vars). Confirm the new muted hexes are intended to be theme-independent (consistent with current production, which also hardcodes them).

compare.designChangeSummary: The git diff against the previous design version is PURELY COSMETIC — no visual, UX, or structural intent changed. All hunks are (a) en-dash to comma substitutions inside comments (e.g. "Bloomberg-style" comment, "Mount-only effect" comment, "hard safety net" comments) and (b) whitespace/spacing reformatting in code that has no runtime effect: `map((it,i)` to `map((it, i)`, `[]` dep arrays now `, []`, `rgba(224,78,31,.18)` to `rgba(224, 78, 31, .18)`, `{threshold:.3}` to `, {threshold:.3}`, SVG path string `${x},${y}` to `${x}, ${y}`, and `0%,100%` to `0%, 100%` in keyframes. The design intent is unchanged: a 7-component tech-effects toolkit — TickerTape (Bloomberg-style scrolling tape, 60s loop, pause-on-hover, up/down colored deltas), SpotlightCard (cursor-following coral radial glow), CountUp (eased count-up with IntersectionObserver + already-on-screen + 800ms safety-timeout triple fallback), Reveal (fade/translate-up on scroll with quadruple fallback incl. 1.5s hard safety net), AnimatedSpark (dash-draw sparkline with gradient area fill + pulsing endpoint), GridBackdrop (faint grid with optional radial mask fade), and Marquee (decorative horizontal scroller).
compare.currentProductionSummary: Production already implements 6 of the 7 design effects as separate "use client" TSX modules under apps/web/src/components/effects/, re-exported from index.ts: TickerTape, SpotlightCard, CountUp, Reveal, AnimatedSpark, GridBackdrop. Each is a faithful, fully-typed port of the design's structure, inline styles, CSS-var theming (var(--ink), var(--paper), var(--up), var(--down), var(--hair), var(--font-mono)), animations (ticker, dashDraw, pulse, spotlight hover), and the load-bearing fallback patterns (Reveal's cancelled-closure + 1.5s safety timer, CountUp's triple fallback). Production additionally HARDENS two things beyond the design for SSR/RSC correctness: (1) CountUp and TickerTape pin `toLocaleString("en-US", ...)` instead of the design's locale-default `toLocaleString(undefined, ...)` to prevent server/client hydration mismatch — a deliberate, documented improvement; (2) AnimatedSpark rounds coordinates via round2() so SSR and client emit identical path strings. The tnum/mono classes are consumed across dashboards-teaser, ai-leaderboard, funding-tracker, and about pages. The only design component not present in production is Marquee.

gaps.description: Marquee (decorative horizontal scrolling row of pillar tags/cities) is defined in the design's effects.jsx but is NOT ported to production. This is the only missing component. Severity is low because Marquee is purely decorative AND is unused even in the design prototype itself — grep confirms it is only defined in effects.jsx and never imported/rendered by any design module. There is no visible feature regression; this is dead code in the design bundle, not active design intent.

Cosmetic-only: production SpotlightCard default color is `rgba(224,78,31,.18)` and GridBackdrop default is `rgba(255,255,255,.05)` (pre-reformat spacing), whereas the reformatted design now writes `rgba(224, 78, 31, .18)` / `rgba(255, 255, 255, .05)`. These are byte-identical to the CSS parser — zero rendered difference. Not actionable beyond optional style-consistency.

recommendedUpdates.change: No functional update required. The design diff is whitespace/comment-only and production already matches the design intent for all 6 components that are actually used. OPTIONAL (skip unless a future surface needs it): port the decorative Marquee component as apps/web/src/components/effects/marquee.tsx and add it to index.ts, mirroring the design's flex row + `@keyframes mq{0%{transform:translateX(0)}100%{transform:translateX(-50%)}}` and the items/speed/style props. Defer until an actual consumer (e.g. a pillar-tag marquee band) exists — building it now would be unused code.

openQuestions: Is the Marquee component intended for any upcoming surface (e.g. a decorative pillar-tag or Asia-cities band)? If not, leave it unported — it is unused in the design prototype too.
Should production backport the design's exact spacing in default rgba color strings for source consistency, or is the functional equivalence acceptable to leave as-is? (Recommend leave as-is.)

compare.designChangeSummary: app.jsx is the design's App root: it owns the hash router, the provider/chrome composition (Header / main / Footer / AuthModal / SearchOverlay / CookieBanner), the CookieBanner component, and three inline pages (ProPage, AwardsPage, NotFound). The git diff vs the previous version shows the design intent moved on three axes. (1) NEW ROUTES were added to the router: `/legal/:slug` -> LegalPage, `/contact` and `/press` -> ContactPage (mode="contact"|"press"), `/about/newsroom` as an `/about` variant, and `/advertise` -> AdvertisePage. `/studio` was upgraded from an inline StaticPage stub to a real StudioPage component. (2) BANNER COLOR SYSTEM CHANGED: CookieBanner and AwardsPage moved from `background: var(--ink); color: var(--paper)` to a new `background: var(--banner); color: #E8EDF7` with hardcoded light-on-navy rgba borders. A new design token `--banner` (#1B2A52 light / #16223C dark) was added in design/project/index.html specifically so these dark banners stay deep-navy in BOTH themes instead of inverting. (3) AwardsPage was massively SIMPLIFIED: the old elaborate hero (440px serif "2026" watermark, triple radial blurs, ◆ ornamental rule, "Est · MMXXVI", gradient-shimmer "Coming this year" headline + awardsShimmer keyframe, italic flourish, coral underline, mail notify chip) was replaced by a calmer hero — a single small "Awards · Inaugural" pill, a plain white serif "Launching next year" headline (no shimmer, no watermark, no keyframe), one short paragraph, one bottom-right accent wash, and "Notify me" / "Back to homepage" buttons. Minor: heading font-weights dropped 700->650, trailing periods removed from ProPage/NotFound headlines, /briefing copy widened from "Asia tech" to "tech across Asia and the world".
compare.currentProductionSummary: Production has already correctly translated the App root into real RSC/Next architecture: the hash-router + provider tree of app.jsx maps to (reader)/layout.tsx (I18nProvider > ThemeProvider > ShellProvider > Header / main / Footer / AuthModal / SearchOverlay / CookieBanner) plus lib/shell.tsx (user, auth/search open state, articlesRead meter, ⌘K handler) — this is faithful and well-structured. Routing is file-system based, so the router switch is replaced by route folders. Production has pages for /, /article/[slug], /search, /dashboards, /newsletters, /account, /trust/[slug], /about, /awards, /briefing, /studio, /[pillar]. NotFound and CookieBanner exist as real components and closely match the PREVIOUS design. However production has NOT picked up the latest design changes: (a) NO /legal, /contact, /press, /advertise routes and NO /about/newsroom variant exist as pages — yet components/footer.tsx ALREADY links to all of them, so every one of those footer links currently 404s; (b) CookieBanner and AwardsPage still use the OLD `var(--ink)`/`var(--paper)` banner coloring, and the `--banner` token is defined NOWHERE in apps/web/src; (c) AwardsPage is still the OLD elaborate shimmer/watermark hero (with the awardsShimmer keyframe), not the simplified version; (d) StudioPage is a real but minimal stub, acceptable but not aligned to the design's upgraded StudioPage.

gaps.description: Dead footer links / missing routes: components/footer.tsx links to /about/newsroom, /contact, /press, /advertise, and /legal/privacy|terms|cookies|gdpr, but none of these have a page in app/(reader). All currently render the 404. The new app.jsx router formalizes these as LegalPage, ContactPage(mode=contact|press), AboutPage(variant=newsroom), and AdvertisePage. This is the highest-impact gap because it is a live broken-nav issue, not just a visual diff.

Dark-mode banner inversion: CookieBanner (components/cookie-banner.tsx) and the Awards hero (app/(reader)/awards/page.tsx) use background:var(--ink) + color:var(--paper). In dark mode production flips those tokens (--paper=#0F172A dark, --ink=#E2E8F0 light), so both banners render light-bg/dark-text — the opposite of intent. The new design adds a fixed --banner token (#1B2A52 / #16223C) with hardcoded #E8EDF7 text precisely to keep these deep-navy in both themes. The token is not defined in globals.css. Note: Tailwind-v4 theming convention says components must never hardcode rgba and must read from var(--...) — the design's switch to a dedicated --banner token is the correct fix for that convention, but the design also hardcodes #E8EDF7/rgba on top of it, which production should mirror with care.

AwardsPage hero is the superseded design. Production still ships the elaborate shimmer/watermark/ornamental hero with the awardsShimmer keyframe and gradient-clip 'Coming this year' headline; the new design replaced it with a calmer 'Launching next year' hero (small inaugural pill, plain white serif headline, one paragraph, single accent wash, Notify me / Back to homepage). Pure copy/visual change, no behavior risk.

Awards invariant 13 tension to verify against the NEW design (not a production gap): invariant 13 requires the year-one Awards page to be a 'Coming this year' SHIMMER hero with no medallion/categories/past winners. The CURRENT production page satisfies the shimmer requirement; the NEW design REMOVES the shimmer and changes the headline to 'Launching next year'. Adopting the new design would drop the shimmer that invariant 13 names. Flagged under invariantConflicts.

StudioPage: production is a minimal stub (kicker + headline + one paragraph + contact box). The new design upgrades /studio to a dedicated StudioPage component (distinct from the /advertise AdvertisePage it was split from). Content of the new StudioPage lives outside app.jsx (not readable here), so the exact target is unknown, but the router intent is that Studio and Advertise are now two separate fuller pages.

Minor copy/weight drift across shared pages: headline font-weight 700->650 (Awards, NotFound, ProPage), trailing periods removed from ProPage and NotFound headlines, /briefing AM/PM copy widened from 'overnight in Asia tech' to 'overnight in tech across Asia and the world'. ProPage is dead code in both design and prod (the /pro route redirects to /), so its drift is cosmetic-only.

This is the prerequisite for the banner-color fix below and resolves the dark-mode inversion at the token layer.

Update CookieBanner to the new banner styling: background:var(--banner), color:#E8EDF7, and the rgba(232,237,247,...) border/divider/text values from the design diff (replacing the var(--ink)/var(--paper) + color-mix borders). Fixes the dark-mode inversion for the cookie banner.

Decide on the Awards hero. If the new design is approved, replace the awards hero with the simplified 'Launching next year' version (background:var(--banner), inaugural pill, plain white serif headline, single paragraph, one bottom-right accent wash, Notify me / Back to homepage) and delete the awardsShimmer keyframe + 2026 watermark + triple blurs. NOTE this drops the shimmer named in invariant 13 — get explicit product sign-off before removing it. If invariant 13 wins, keep the shimmer hero but still migrate its background from var(--ink) to var(--banner) to fix dark mode.

Create the missing reader routes that footer.tsx already links to so they stop 404-ing: app/(reader)/legal/[slug]/page.tsx (privacy|terms|cookies|gdpr), app/(reader)/contact/page.tsx and app/(reader)/press/page.tsx (or one contact route taking a mode/segment), and app/(reader)/advertise/page.tsx. Match the design's ContactPage/LegalPage/AdvertisePage intent (their bodies live in other design src files, not app.jsx, so pull copy from those).

Add an /about/newsroom variant. The design routes /about/newsroom to AboutPage(variant="newsroom") and /about to the trust variant. Production /about is a single rich page with no variant. Either add a newsroom variant (read a route segment / searchParam and branch the AboutPage), or repoint the footer 'Newsroom' link to /about until the variant is built. Lowest-risk interim: change the footer link target.

Flesh out StudioPage to the design's upgraded version (now separate from Advertise). Low priority; current stub is functional and on-message about top-to-bottom disclosure. Pull the real Studio copy from the design's dedicated source module once located.

invariantConflicts: Invariant 13 (Awards year-one = 'Coming this year' SHIMMER hero): the NEW design removes the gradient shimmer + the awardsShimmer keyframe and changes the headline to 'Launching next year'. Production currently HAS the shimmer (compliant). Adopting the new Awards design would drop the shimmer the invariant explicitly names. The new hero still has no medallion / no categories / no past winners, so those clauses remain satisfied — only the 'shimmer' clause is in tension. Needs product sign-off before removing the shimmer; reconcile invariant 13 wording if the calmer hero is chosen.

Invariant 7 / Tailwind-v4 theming convention (no hardcoded rgba; read from var(--...)): the design's banner fix introduces hardcoded #E8EDF7 text and rgba(232,237,247,...) borders on top of the new --banner token. This is acceptable for a fixed-navy surface that must NOT adapt to dark mode (that is the whole point), but it does deviate from the 'never hardcode rgba' rule, so call it out when implementing rather than letting it drift silently. No brand-color values from invariant 7 are violated.
openQuestions: Awards: keep the invariant-13 shimmer hero, or adopt the calmer 'Launching next year' hero from the new design? This needs an explicit product decision because it touches a stated invariant.

The new pages' actual content (LegalPage, ContactPage, AdvertisePage, StudioPage, AboutPage newsroom variant) is defined in design source files OTHER than app.jsx (app.jsx only wires the routes). Which design src modules hold those bodies, and should each be a separate comparison pass before building the production routes?

Should /contact and /press be two separate route folders (matching the file-system router) or one route that branches on a mode/segment (matching the design's single ContactPage with mode prop)? Affects the route layout in the recommendation above.
Is the --banner token (#1B2A52 / #16223C) approved for adoption into globals.css as a first-class DTW token, given it is not among the invariant-7 fixed brand colors?

compare.designChangeSummary: The new design/project/src/i18n.jsx is identical to production EXCEPT one line in PILLAR_I18N: the `asia` pillar entry (`vi: "Châu Á", id: "Asia"`) is replaced by a `latest` pillar entry (`vi: "Mới nhất", id: "Terbaru"`). This is the i18n footprint of a much larger, design-wide pillar rename in data.jsx: the `asia` pillar (label "Asia", slug "/asia", color var(--asia)) becomes the `latest` pillar (label "Latest", slug "/latest", SAME color var(--asia)). All mock stories that were `pillar:"asia"` are re-distributed to existing pillars (dev/ai/policy/startups), newsletters AM/PM Brief move to `pillar:"latest"`, and two new newsletters (Deep Dive, DTW Awards) are added. The translator API (useT/useLang/I18nProvider), NAV_I18N, and date-formatting helpers are unchanged in intent. Net: rename the lead/coral pillar from a geographic identity ("Asia") to an editorial-recency identity ("Latest"), keeping the same coral accent color. The i18n table is just one of ~6 code surfaces that hardcode the `asia` pillar id.
compare.currentProductionSummary: Production apps/web/src/lib/i18n.tsx is a faithful, well-typed mirror of the design's prior i18n.jsx: same I18nProvider (localStorage "dtw-lang" + document lang attr, with an extra SSR-safe hydrate-after-mount), same useT(en,vi,id) inline translator, same NAV_I18N table, and superior date formatting (adds explicit Asia/Singapore timeZone to prevent SSR/CSR drift). It still carries the OLD `asia` entry in PILLAR_I18N (line 86: `asia: { vi: "Châu Á", id: "Asia" }`). The `asia` pillar id is hardcoded as a literal in the PillarId union and PILLARS table in lib/data.ts (line 5, 18) AND packages/ui/src/pillars.ts (line 5, 17), in article-view.ts asPillarId() switch + default fallback "asia" (lines 64/70) and the "var(--asia)"/"Asia" string fallbacks, in the Payload Pillars.ts admin descriptions (the literal slug enumeration "ai|startups|asia|dev|products|policy" and icon list), and as CSS tokens --asia / --color-asia in globals.css (--asia = #E04E1F, the brand coral). Pillars themselves are real Payload CMS entities (payload-server.ts queries the `pillars` collection by slug), so the on-screen label/slug ultimately comes from the CMS, while these code tables are the frontend mirror used for typing, colors, icons, and i18n chrome. There is also a homepage component named asia-spotlight.tsx and an `asia` icon — both named after the old pillar identity.

gaps.description: PILLAR_I18N in apps/web/src/lib/i18n.tsx still maps key `asia` (vi 'Châu Á' / id 'Asia'); the design replaced it with `latest` (vi 'Mới nhất' / id 'Terbaru'). With the production PillarId type still being `asia`, the localized label for the renamed pillar would have no vi/id translation and would fall back to English — broken i18n chrome for the lead pillar.

The pillar rename asia→latest is a taxonomy-identity change that spans far beyond i18n: PillarId union type and PILLARS table in lib/data.ts, the duplicate PillarId/PILLARS in packages/ui/src/pillars.ts, asPillarId() switch + default fallback in article-view.ts, the slug enumeration in payload/collections/Pillars.ts admin copy, and the homepage asia-spotlight component naming. i18n alone cannot be updated coherently without these.

The CMS `pillars` collection is the source of truth for pillar slug/label (invariant #8). A code-only edit to i18n/data tables does not change the actual rendered pillar unless the Payload pillar row's slug is migrated from `asia` to `latest` and its title updated to 'Latest'. Without a CMS data migration + route revalidation, the /asia route and 'Asia' label persist regardless of code-table edits.

Editorial repositioning: design moves all stories off the `asia` (now `latest`) pillar into dev/ai/policy/startups, and changes newsletter copy ('What broke overnight in tech across Asia and the world') plus adds a 'DTW Awards' newsletter. Production data.ts mock + newsletter copy still references Asia-centric framing. Mock-data differences are not gaps per se, but the newsletter list and DTW Awards newsletter are real CMS-config-shaped content the design now expects.

The renamed pillar keeps color var(--asia) = #E04E1F, which is the brand coral accent (invariant #7). A pillar literally named `latest` mapped to a CSS token named `--asia` is now a naming mismatch that will confuse future maintenance (the token/value can stay, but the name reads wrong).

recommendedUpdates.change: In PILLAR_I18N, replace the `asia: { vi: 'Châu Á', id: 'Asia' }` entry with `latest: { vi: 'Mới nhất', id: 'Terbaru' }` to match the design. Must be done in lockstep with the PillarId type rename below so the Record<PillarId,...> stays valid.

Rename the `asia` pillar to `latest` in the shared PillarId union and PILLARS table: id 'asia'→'latest', label 'Asia'→'Latest', slug '/asia'→'/latest', keep color var(--asia). Update the duplicate definition in packages/ui too. Also update PILLAR_ICONS key and any newsletter/podcast mock entries that key off 'asia'.

Update asPillarId() switch case and the default fallback from 'asia' to 'latest', and the string fallbacks 'var(--asia)'/'Asia' label so unknown/missing pillars resolve to the renamed lead pillar consistently.

Update the Payload Pillars collection admin copy: change the slug help text enumeration 'ai|startups|asia|dev|products|policy' to use 'latest', and the icon-name list if the 'asia' icon is renamed. NOTE: this is only the admin description — the actual pillar slug/title lives in CMS data and requires a Payload data migration (update the pillar row slug 'asia'→'latest', title 'Asia'→'Latest') plus revalidateTag for the pillars taxonomy and affected routes. Coordinate with invariant #1 (writes via Payload API/afterChange hooks) and #8 (CMS-driven routes).

Optional cleanup for naming coherence: consider renaming the CSS token --asia/--color-asia to --latest (or a neutral --lead) since the pillar is no longer 'Asia'. Value stays #E04E1F coral. Touches every consumer of var(--asia). Defer if churn outweighs benefit; the simplest safe path is to keep --asia as the token name and only rename the pillar id.

Decide the fate of the homepage 'Asia Spotlight' component (asia-spotlight.tsx) and the 'asia' icon. The design keeps an 'Asia, Decoded' podcast and Asia-framed copy, so the Asia editorial concept survives as a section even though the pillar is renamed — likely NO rename needed for the component, but confirm the homepage band still pulls the right pillar id after the rename.

invariantConflicts: Invariant #8 (Pillar/Sub-section/Tag are CMS entities; routes regenerate from CMS): the asia→latest rename cannot be a code-table-only change. The actual pillar slug/label is stored in the Payload `pillars` collection and drives routes/sitemap/RSS. Renaming requires a CMS data migration (pillar row slug 'asia'→'latest', title 'Asia'→'Latest') with revalidateTag, NOT just edits to lib/i18n.tsx / lib/data.ts. The code tables must be kept in sync with that CMS write.

Invariant #9 (i18n year1 = en/id/vi; never hardcode locale lists): not violated by this change (the translator and locale handling are unchanged and correct), but worth noting the locale tables remain hardcoded en/vi/id as designed for Year 1. The PILLAR_I18N edit must preserve both vi and id keys for the new `latest` entry ('Mới nhất' / 'Terbaru') so no locale silently falls back to English.

Invariant #7 (brand color coral accent #E04E1F): the renamed pillar retains var(--asia)=#E04E1F. No color change, but if --asia is renamed to --latest the literal value must remain #E04E1F to preserve the brand coral.
openQuestions: Is the asia→latest rename an intentional product/editorial decision (repositioning the lead pillar from a geographic 'Asia' identity to an editorial 'Latest/most-recent' identity), or just a design-prototype experiment? This determines whether to schedule the cross-cutting code + CMS migration at all.

Should the existing CMS pillar row be migrated in place (slug asia→latest, preserving article relationships) or should 'latest' be a new pillar with 'asia' archived? In-place rename preserves article links but needs a redirect from /asia to /latest for SEO.

Does the 'Asia' editorial concept fully disappear, or does it survive as a section/spotlight (the design keeps 'Asia, Decoded' podcast and Asia-framed AM Brief copy)? If it survives only as a homepage band, the asia-spotlight component and icon likely stay as-is.
Should the CSS token --asia be renamed to --latest for coherence, or left as a stable token name (value #E04E1F) to avoid large find/replace churn across all var(--asia) consumers?

The design adds a 'DTW Awards' newsletter and a 'Deep Dive' newsletter and changes AM/PM Brief copy — are these CMS-config content changes the team wants reflected, or out of scope for the i18n surface review?

compare.designChangeSummary: The design file design/project/src/studio.jsx is UNTRACKED/new (git diff prints nothing; status shows '??'), so the ENTIRE file is new design intent with no prior version to diff against. The design specifies a full DTW Studio (branded-content) marketing page with four blocks: (1) a dark relative hero with GridBackdrop, kicker 'DTW Studio', a 52px serif headline ('Storytelling for brands, held to a newsroom standard'), a long lede paragraph, and a coral 'Start a project' mailto CTA with a mail icon; (2) a 'What we make' section — a 3-column services grid (Branded features/feather, Research & data/data, Audio video & events/headphone) where each card has a rounded brand-navy icon tile, a serif title, and a description; (3) a 'How we work' disclosure-principles panel (surface-2 background) with an h2 'Sponsored, and never hidden', an intro paragraph, a 3-column principles grid (Always labelled/check, Separate from the newsroom/lock, Clear on the line/info) each with an accent icon chip, and a footer link to /trust/sponsored ('Read the full Sponsored & Affiliate policy' with arrow-r); (4) a 2-column CTA band ('Have a story worth telling well?' + a full-width coral mailto button showing the partnership email and a fine-print line). Container maxWidth 980, paddingTop 32 / paddingBottom 72. Every string is fully localized via the t(en, vi, id) helper. All CTAs route to a mailto to partnership@dailytechwire.com. Crucially, the design re-states the sponsored-disclosure rules in prose (yellow background, 'Paid Partner' tag, disclosure top/middle/end; Studio is a separate team; partners can't approve copy naming competitors).

No hero/GridBackdrop, no services grid, no principles panel, no CTA band, no mailto links, no localization. CSS vars --surface, --surface-2, --hair, --hair-2, --ink-2, --accent, --accent-ink, --ink, --paper all exist in app/globals.css.

gaps.description: Production renders a 40-line placeholder; the new design is a full 4-section page (dark hero with GridBackdrop, 3-card 'What we make' services grid, 'How we work' disclosure-principles panel, and a 2-column CTA band). The entire hero, services grid, principles panel, and CTA band are missing in production.

No internationalization in production. The current studio page is English-only and not a client component, so it cannot use useT as written.

The design references two CSS variables that DO NOT exist anywhere in production: --banner (hero background) and --brand-navy (service icon tile background + icon color). Container/layout mismatch: design uses maxWidth 980 and paddingBottom 72; production uses maxWidth 800 and paddingBottom 64. Minor but worth aligning when rebuilding.

The design links to /trust/sponsored for the full Sponsored & Affiliate policy. Production studio has no such link. The /trust/[slug] route exists, but confirm a 'sponsored' slug entry is actually published in the CMS/data so the link is not a dead end.

recommendedUpdates.change: Rewrite apps/web/src/app/(reader)/studio/page.tsx as a 'use client' component (matching about/awards/newsletters), importing useT/useLang from @/lib/i18n, Icon from @/components/icons, and GridBackdrop from @/components/effects. (about page, and the project invariant names 'Asia Press Corporation'). Verify a 'sponsored' Sponsored & Affiliate policy entry exists under the /trust/[slug] route (data/CMS) so the design's 'Read the full Sponsored & Affiliate policy' link resolves; if missing, either point the link at the correct existing slug or flag for content creation.

invariantConflicts: Invariant #11 (NO logo badge in header / design bundle ships dtw-logo-primary.svg): NO conflict from this file — studio.jsx does not import or render any logo/badge; it only uses the wordmark-free Studio page chrome. No action needed here, but noted because the prompt asked to flag logo reintroduction.

Naming (not a numbered invariant but a source-of-truth conflict): design's 'Asia Press Centre Group' contradicts production/context 'Asia Press Corporation (Pte. Ltd.)'. Use the production name. The about/all-context docs are authoritative; the design copy here is wrong and should not be ported verbatim.
openQuestions: Which email + domain is canonical for Studio partnership inquiries — production's studio@dailytechwire.asia, the design's partnership@dailytechwire.com, or a new partnership@dailytechwire.asia? (Recommend the .asia domain to match the rest of the site.)
Should the parent-org line read 'Asia Press Corporation' (production/invariant) and drop the design's 'Asia Press Centre Group' wording?
Does the team want a dedicated studio-hero color (define --banner / --brand-navy tokens) or is reusing var(--ink)/var(--accent) acceptable (recommended, lower risk)?
Is there a published 'sponsored' entry under /trust/[slug] so the 'Read the full Sponsored & Affiliate policy' link resolves, or should it point at a different existing slug?

Should the rebuilt page also export Next.js metadata (title/description) for SEO? Sibling client reader pages currently do not, so this would be a deliberate enhancement rather than matching the existing convention.

The closest existing page is /studio (studio/page.tsx), which is a bare ~40-line placeholder (kicker + headline + one disclosure note, partnership enquiries to studio@dailytechwire.asia) — it does NOT contain the rate-card/formats/audience content the design assigns to /advertise. plan.executiveSummary: A large Claude Design update lands a coherent brand refresh plus several new pages, but it mixes safe parity work with three decision-gated invariant conflicts that must be resolved before EXECUTE. FOUNDATION FIRST: the design introduces four new CSS tokens (--banner #1B2A52/#16223C, --brand-navy #1B2A52/#E2E8F0, --amber #F59E0B, --brand-amber #D4623C), lightens --paper to #FDFCF8, re-tones all six pillar colors to a muted earthy set, and swaps the editorial typeface from Source Serif 4 (serif) to Schibsted Grotesk (variable sans). Almost every per-surface gap is blocked on these tokens existing in apps/web/src/app/globals.css, so they go first as pure additive changes (the @theme --color-* mappings are var() refs that auto-follow). Three hard invariant conflicts require product sign-off before any code: (#11) the design reintroduces a navy DTW monogram header logo badge and ships dtw-logo-primary.svg — currently production is COMPLIANT (text wordmark only), so do NOT build the badge without an explicit invariant amendment; (#7) the design softens the coral accent #E04E1F -> #D4623C site-wide, contradicting the fixed-color invariant; (#4) the paywall '3' threshold remains hardcoded and must read from CMS. One GOOD invariant alignment is already overdue: production is presently OUT OF COMPLIANCE with #5 because it still renders the AI-ASSISTED badge and three kind=\"ai\" disclosure boxes — the new design correctly removes them, so the parity work fixes a live violation. The largest behavioral change is the asia->latest pillar rename, which is a CMS data migration under invariant #8, not a code-only edit. New pages /advertise, /contact, /press, /legal/[slug], and /about/newsroom are HIGH priority because footer.tsx already links all of them and they currently 404. Recommended order: tokens+typeface foundation -> invariant-#5 AI-disclosure removal -> global chrome (header/footer/section rules, ui primitives) -> homepage -> article/pillar -> dashboards -> secondary pages (about/trust/awards/account/newsletters) -> new pages -> asia->latest CMS rename as a separate gated workstream.

globalFoundationChanges.change: Add the four new design tokens to :root (--banner #1B2A52, --brand-navy #1B2A52, --amber #F59E0B, --brand-amber #D4623C) and the dark-mode overrides to html[data-theme="dark"] (--banner #16223C, --brand-navy #E2E8F0). Optionally expose --color-banner / --color-brand-navy in the @theme inline block so Tailwind utilities (bg-banner) exist with the same mapping convention as --color-paper/--color-ink. Pure additive — nothing references them yet, so zero regression risk. This single change unblocks header, footer, homepage banners (AsiaSpotlight, NewsletterCta), section-header rules, about/studio/contact/advertise/legal heroes, dashboards header, awards hero, cookie banner, related-row border, and the Button primary variant.

globalFoundationChanges.rationale: This is the single highest-visual-impact change — it alters every headline, byline, blockquote, and article-prose heading site-wide. It is foundational because article-body and all heading-weight tweaks depend on the final typeface, but it is DECISION-GATED (open question) since it changes the entire typographic voice from serif to sans. globalFoundationChanges.rationale: This is the cleanest example of a decision that must be made at the token layer before any surface work, because dozens of components inherit --accent. It cannot be applied silently — invariant #7 names #E04E1F explicitly.
